Send a short note about something you’ve built. We look forward to meeting you.

About DiligenceSquared 

 DiligenceSquared (YC F25) is rebuilding how private equity firms, hedge funds, private credit, and corporates conduct commercial due diligence. We deploy AI agents to automate expert interviews at scale, synthesize findings, and deliver fully traceable CDD reports – at a fraction of the cost and timeline of traditional consulting. Backed by Y Combinator and Relentless, we're a small, high-output team working on some of the most intellectually demanding problems in private equity research.

 The Role 

 We're looking for a sharp, client-facing Business Lead to own the delivery of commercial due diligence for leading private equity funds.

 You'll be the human-in-the-loop at the heart of our AI-powered research process: translating client needs into precise guidelines for our AI agents, overseeing the quality of their outputs, and ensuring every engagement is delivered with white-glove service.

 This is a highly visible, client-facing role. You'll work directly with partners and deal teams at top-tier PE funds, have real ownership over your work, and help shape how we build and scale the product – without the layers of process that come with larger organizations.

 What You'll Do 

 
 Own project delivery across multiple simultaneous CDD engagements, from kickoff through final report

 Manage client relationships day-to-day

 Coordinate expert sourcing across expert networks

 Work closely with our AI systems to oversee interviews, analysis and synthesis to ensure coverage against client briefs

 Partner with our engineering team to translate client and operational insights into product improvements

 Build and refine internal processes and templates that improve delivery quality and speed

 Collaborate with the founding team on product feedback

 
 What We're Looking For 

 
 3–6 years of experience at an MBB firm, in private equity, or in investment banking

 • Experience with commercial due diligence — you understand the methodology, the client expectations, and what separates a good CDD from a great one

 Track record of managing complex, deadline-driven workstreams with multiple stakeholders

 Exceptional written and verbal communication

 High attention to detail — you catch errors others miss and hold your work to an exceptionally high bar

 High ownership mentality: you spot problems early and solve them independently

 Genuine curiosity about AI and its implications for knowledge work — you should be excited about working at the cutting edge of how AI is transforming research and analysis

 
 Start-up mentality — you're energized by ownership, speed, and variety; equally comfortable shaping strategy and handling the unglamorous work that comes with building something from the ground up

 Comfortable operating in ambiguity and building structure where there isn't any

 
 Why DiligenceSquared 

 
 Early-stage equity at a YC-backed company with strong investor conviction and active deal flow

 Work at the cutting edge of applied AI — you'll use and shape tools that don't exist anywhere else in the industry

 Work directly with the founding team — high ownership and real impact from day one

 Intellectually demanding work: complex industries, sophisticated clients, high stakes

 A front-row seat to how AI is transforming the multi-billion dollar commercial due diligence market

 Competitive compensation and a collaborative, no-bureaucracy culture

You'll own the frontend for AI-powered research workflows - building interfaces for voice AI interactions and interactive data visualization to create the next-generation of market reports for investment professionals in Private Equity, Hedge Funds, Private Credit, and Corporate M&A.

 About DiligenceSquared 

 DiligenceSquared automates commercial due diligence for Private Equity funds. We're YC-backed, just raised a significant Seed round, and our clients include some of the world's largest PE funds.

 The market is $10B+ annually - PE funds pay McKinsey, BCG, and Bain $500K-$1M per report. Our AI agents do this work faster, cheaper, and with full auditability (every claim linked to source).

 Our founders bought and sold these reports at Blackstone and BCG where they held senior positions. We have deep experience with the workflows and know exactly what the market needs - and now we're automating it.

 What you’ll do  

 
 Own the entire frontend experience for our AI-powered platform, creating interfaces that delight users at top investment firms

 Transform complex data and AI outputs into intuitive, actionable interfaces

 Build performant, scalable frontend architecture that can handle intensive real-time operations

 Push the boundaries of what's possible with modern web technologies

 Apply learnings from each project into the product, so we're 1% better everyday (Law of Compounding is real. After all, we are founded by investment professionals)

 
 Qualifications: 

 
 3+ years of professional React/TypeScript experience

 Deep expertise in modern frontend architecture and state management

 Experience building complex, data-heavy applications

 Experience with Next.js or similar React frameworks

 Portfolio demonstrating craft and attention to detail

 Passion for UI/UX and making complex information accessible

 
 Bonus: 

 
 Background building interfaces for AI/ML products

 Experience with real-time communication features

 Familiarity with voice AI integrations

 Previous startup experience

 
 Who you are  

 
 Obsess over craft - every interaction and layout decision matters

 Take pride in your code and want to work with others who do the same 

 Act with Urgency so we can empower you with Agency 

 Pay high attention to detail and are curious about how great products work 

 Feel discomfort when things don't move as fast as you feel they should

Diode Computers, Inc. is looking for a senior software engineer to help us fix circuit board design and manufacturing.

 The team has shipped real hardware at Apple, Anduril, and several med-tech startups; now we’re focusing our attention on helping others build hardware better. We’ve raised $14M and were featured on the a16z “American Dynamism 50” list this year. Our customers include some of the biggest names in robotics, health-tech, and defense.

 Since we make software for hardware, you'll learn lots about the world of building physical things, even if you haven't tinkered with electronics before. You'll quickly pick up the basics of electrical engineering and PCB design, and we expect you to bring a strong software-first lens as we build tools for our customers and in-house EE team.

 We work in a fast, engineering-driven culture. We iterate on ideas quickly, and expect you to take ownership over what you build. You'll make decisions on everything from technical implementation to UX, and drive projects through from idea to production.

 What you'll do

 We move fast, so expect each week to look a little different than the last. That said, here’s a sampling of projects we have/will work on, to give you a taste for the kinds of problems we think about.

 
 Designing a programming language for PCB schematics that is intuitive for both humans and LLMs to read and write

 Rust compiler and CLI build tool for compiling schematics and interacting with the rest of our infrastructure

 API integrations with PCB fabs for real-time quoting and ordering

 Constraint-based layout algorithm for automatically arranging components on a PCB

 Auto-generated electrical schematics visualization

 AI agents to automate various steps of the design process, building on our bespoke tooling

 Collaborate with our in-house EE team to ruthlessly optimize their workflow

 Contribute to open source libraries, and publish our own (more here soon)

 
 We work mostly in Rust and TypeScript.

 What you'll need

 
 A few years of experience with at least one of {Rust, Python, TypeScript}

 Some prior experience owning large parts of a technical project

 Excitement to work hard on hard technical problems

 The ability to work from our office in NYC, ~5 days a week

 Bonus points:
 
 You've tinkered with hardware before (even small hobby projects). If not, you’ll learn on the job!

 Experience with building robust AI tools (agents, evals, the whole shebang)

 


 
 What you'll get

 
 A competitive salary and equity grant

 Free food and the occasional team offsite

 Great healthcare (incl. vision, dental), PTO, and other benefits

 Relocation support to NYC, if you need it

Come build circuit boards, using AI agents.

 We are building zener , a code first approach to PCB design, with integrated DFM and CAM to go from design → manufacturing on the same platform. We design boards for the robotics, aerospace and medical industry, with fortune 100 clients who need power electronics and HDI designs.

About the Role 

 Draftwise is growing its implementation function to support an expanding enterprise customer base across EMEA. We're looking for an Implementation Specialist based in London to own the technical onboarding experience for new customers from contract signature through a live, production-ready deployment. This role is the EMEA counterpart to an existing function on our US team. The playbooks and processes are established -- you'll own the EMEA customer base and be the accountable point of contact in the timezone.

 What You'll Do 

 
 
 Own implementations end-to-end: internal handoff, kickoff, environment setup, DMS/SSO/add-in configuration, document ingestion, user registration, and CS handoff



 
 Owns outcomes, not just tasks -- when something's broken, fixes it; when something's missing, builds it



 
 Serve as the primary customer-facing POC through platform readiness for Scale accounts; co-own the technical track alongside CSMs on Enterprise accounts



 
 Coordinate with IT contacts at law firms on identity configuration, DMS indexing, and add-in deployment (iManage, NetDocuments, SharePoint, Microsoft Office)



 
 Troubleshoot integration issues, escalate to Engineering with clear problem statements, and close the loop with customers



 
 Maintain accurate implementation status in Salesforce and Linear; keep internal stakeholders informed without requiring them to ask



 
 Contribute to playbooks and documentation that make the implementation motion repeatable



 
 Surface friction from implementations back to the team -- on the product, the process, or both



 
 What We're Looking For 

 
 
 3+ years in implementation, solutions engineering, or technical onboarding at a B2B SaaS company



 
 Comfortable navigating technical configurations (SSO, identity providers, cloud storage, enterprise software deployment) without needing Engineering in the room



 
 Strong communicator -- you can run an IT working session and a stakeholder status call in the same week



 
 You build structure where there isn't any, and don't wait to be told what to do



 
 Good judgment about when to escalate vs. when to figure it out yourself



 
 Based in London or able to work UK hours



 
 Nice to Have 

 
 
 Experience with legal technology or law firm IT environments



 
 Familiarity with document management systems (iManage, NetDocuments, SharePoint)



 
 Experience working cross-functionally with Sales, CS, and Product at an early-stage company



 
 Why This Role 

 Small team, real ownership, concrete outcomes. Customers go live because of you. There's room to shape how this function scales as Draftwise grows across EMEA.

As a Legal Product Specialist you will be the internal voice-of-the-customer at DraftWise. You will work closely with our Product, Design and Engineering teams in the development and testing of new features and you will help shape our understanding of drafting, negotiation and review workflows for lawyers. You will also help communicate the value of our product to new users and customers.

 In order to succeed in this role, you will need to be an excellent communicator, capable of understanding technical concepts and translating complex legal concepts into terms non-lawyers can interpret.

 Your day-to-day work will be a mix of:

 
 Keeping up-to-date with fast moving AI and legal industry trends

 Identifying truly useful applications of LLMs to the legal industry based off of your own knowledge and through interviews with other legal professionals

 Working closely with the Product team to define the vision for new DraftWise applications

 Testing new DraftWise applications and providing detailed feedback to the Design and Engineering teams

 Validating the output of generative AI models and providing labeled data to assist with the fine-tuning of such models

 Joining user and sales calls to help communicate the value of DraftWise to fellow legal professionals

 
 We are a small team and expect all members to flexibly help wherever the company needs them the most - if you have an idea for what DraftWise needs, we want to empower you to pursue that project. You will have broad ownership over your initiatives, the ability to shape our culture as we grow and flexibility over your schedule..

 What we value

 
 Strong communication skills in an open environment.

 Creating an environment of trust through well-intentioned feedback.

 Skills to work independently and make decisions with minimal supervision.

 Interest in working in a dynamic environment with dynamic objectives.

 
 About You

 
 5-8 years experience practicing as a transactional lawyer in Big Law

 Experience drafting, negotiating and reviews legal agreements

 Outstanding communication skills and a flair for breaking complex legal concepts down into terms non-lawyers can understand

 Basic understanding of software and technical products and an eagerness to learn more about product development and the application of AI in the legal industry

 Passion for building a product to help legal professionals

 Technical experience as a software engineer or similar role is a nice-to-have

 In-house experience is a nice-to-have

 
 What we offer

 
 All-remote work style, work anywhere. Ideally within UTC+4 to UTC-7

 Equity plan

 Competitive salary

 Private medical care

 New laptop and a work-from-home stipend for necessary accessories

 Generous PTO / sick leave

 Opportunity to lead our company as we disrupt the legal world

About DraftWise

 DraftWise is building an innovative knowledge platform for the legal industry. Our core product unlocks for the first time the years of historic legal contracts that firms have negotiated for their clients. We use the latest in data management, machine learning and language analytics to help lawyers and legal professionals write better, safer contracts.

 Our founding team is composed of former Palantir and Google engineers and an experienced lawyer from a top global law firm. Our highly talented team is designed to focus on building and delivering the best product in the industry, with the engineering, product and customer expertise to fulfill this promise. We are a fully remote company, though offer the option to cowork in our major cities in North America and Europe. Our global culture means we hire only the best from around the world and value people who are respectful of their colleagues, who support each other to achieve our shared goals and who pride themselves on delivering high-quality work.

 While an early stage company, we are backed by Y-Combinator, Index Ventures, and leading VCfunds and are generating revenue from some of the biggest names in the industry, members of the Vault 10, Magic Circle, Am Law 100 and other rankings of law firms. Our company is growing extremely fast, with customers across multiple countries and more to come.

 You will help our team grow with this rising demand and have a real impact on the most important law firms in the world.

 About the Role

 As a Senior Customer Success Manager on our team, you will play a critical role in ensuring our customers achieve their desired outcomes and realize the full value of our platform. You are directly responsible for the success of these customers, through onboarding, driving adoption, and reducing churn. You will act as the voice of your users in sharing their feedback across the organization, and defining and proposing new product features to enhance DraftWise’s impact on our customers..

 We are quickly growing the number of customers we are working with so you will be instrumental in designing scalable operations, identifying ways to better support more customers over time and selecting the tools you need to do the job well.

 We are a small team and expect all members to flexibly help wherever the company needs them the most - if you have an idea for what DraftWise needs, we want to empower you to pursue that project. You will have broad ownership over your initiatives, the ability to shape our culture as we grow and flexibility over your schedule. We will push you to develop a portfolio of customers all using DraftWise, sharing ideas for its improvement and being the voice of your users.

 What we value

 
 Strong communication skills in an open environment.

 Creating an environment of trust through well-intentioned feedback.

 Skills to work independently and make decisions with minimal supervision.

 Interest in working in a dynamic environment with dynamic objectives.

 
 About You

 We're looking for employees who have:

 
 Exceptional communication skills with an emphasis on building trust

 Proven track record of driving customer engagement, retention, and expansion

 Passion for seeing customers succeed with the product you helped build

 Passion for building communities of evangelists amongst our customers

 Experience designing software training and analyzing cohort retention

 Interest in the legal industry or in language analytics

 6+ years in customer-facing roles in a SaaS environment

 
 It's also great if you have:

 
 Interest in designing scalable company processes from an early stage

 Experience in the legaltech industry

 Experience upselling customers on new features and products

 Interest in product management and user experience design

 Comfort with data analytics and reporting

 
 What we offer

 
 Flexible remote work - with a hybrid option if desired

 Equity plan

 Competitive salary

 Private medical care

 New laptop and a work-from-home stipend for necessary accessories

 Generous PTO / sick leave

 Opportunity to lead our company as we disrupt the legal world

About Us 

 Founded in 2021, Draftwise is a contract drafting, review, and negotiation AI powered by your legal knowledge. Draftwise executes complex drafting, review, and search workflows directly within Microsoft Word, drawing from your organization's best guidance, precedent, and language.

 Draftwise serves top law firms and legal departments globally, including over half the Vault 10, dozens of Am Law 100 firms, and Fortune 500 organizations. The company is headquartered in New York and has offices in London.

 Join Our Mission 

 Draftwise is building AI that makes contract work faster and more precise for the world's leading law firms. Our platform unlocks institutional knowledge from decades of legal work, helping lawyers draft and negotiate with greater efficiency and accuracy.

 You'll work alongside experienced engineers from companies like Palantir and Google, plus legal professionals from top global firms, building technology that elite lawyers use daily.

 Why Join Draftwise 

 **Proven Product-Market Fit 
**Our core technology serves the legal industry's most demanding clients. You'll build solutions that lawyers at Vault 10, Magic Circle, and AmLaw 100 firms rely on for their most important work.

 **Strong Foundation 
**Y Combinator alumni with $20M Series A funding from Index Ventures. Work with the latest generative AI and machine learning technology while having the resources to execute ambitious projects.

 **Meaningful Impact 
**Your work directly improves how legal professionals serve their clients. Build features that users actively love and depend on, with immediate feedback on real-world impact.

 Global Offices & Remote Culture 

 Offices in New York, London, and San Francisco with the possibility of remote work. Work with a global team while maintaining the flexibility that works best for you.

 What we value

 
 Strong communication skills in an open environment.

 The ability to work independently and make informed decisions with minimal supervision.

 Interest in working in a dynamic environment with dynamic objectives.

 A commitment to autonomy, ownership, and delivering high-quality solutions.

 Openness to giving and receiving constructive feedback.

 
 About this role

 We are seeking an experienced Senior Enterprise Solutions Architect to take a leadership role in shaping DraftWise’s technical strategy and serving as a subject matter expert (SME) in our deployment infrastructure, information security stance, and third-party integrations. This role will involve driving pre-sales engagement, leading enterprise rollout strategies, and ensuring ongoing customer success, with a strong focus on solving complex technical challenges and delivering scalable, impactful solutions that align with our customers’ strategic goals.

 You will regularly interact with C-level executives, partners, and directors, requiring exceptional communication and presentation skills to effectively engage senior stakeholders.

 Key Responsibilities:

 
 
 Technical Pre-Sales Leadership: 
 
 Guide customer leadership in understanding the differences between on-premise and cloud deployment options, effectively communicating the benefits of cloud adoption to support their long-term success.

 Lead infrastructure discussions with sponsors during pre-sales and pre-conversion phases.

 Oversee technical proof of concepts (PoCs) for prospective customers and address infrastructure-related inquiries.

 Articulate security considerations, deployment trade-offs, and the operational impact of different architectures, including when using Large Language Models (LLMs).

 


 
 Enterprise Rollout Strategy: 
 
 Spearhead infrastructure planning and discussions to transition pilot programs into full-scale enterprise rollouts.

 Take ownership of Document Management System (DMS) integration conversations, providing clarity and direction for successful implementation.

 Engage senior stakeholders to articulate the value and technical architecture of rollout strategies.

 


 
 Migration Technical Leadership: 
 
 Oversee customer migrations from DraftWise On-Premise to Cloud, ensuring seamless transitions and alignment with enterprise goals.

 Strategically lead and advise on migrations of customer third-party systems, such as iManage, from on-premise to cloud environments, ensuring minimal disruption and optimal outcomes.

 


 
 Strategic Upsell Enablement: 
 
 Drive the strategy, design, and scope of third-party integration opportunities, including assessing customer needs, identifying appropriate solutions, and collaborating with engineering teams to implement integrations (e.g. Document Management Systems (DMS), Customer Relationship Management (CRM) platforms, and other enterprise tools.

 Collaborate on custom development projects, providing technical expertise during the sales and design phases.

 


 
 Information Security (Infosec): 
 
 Address technical and security concerns or questions with client CSO/Director of Infosec.

 


 
 Stretch Goals: 
 
 Design and build cloud infrastructure stacks tailored to specific enterprise needs.

 Develop innovative cloud solutions to address unique customer requests and challenges.

 


 
 About you

 We're looking for teammates who possess:

 
 Strong problem-solving skills with a customer-centric mindset and the ability to think quickly on your feet.

 Exceptional verbal, written, and presentation communication skills, with a proven ability to engage C-level executives, partners, and directors effectively.

 Proven experience in customer-facing roles, with a strong focus on technical pre-sales and rollout support.

 A solid understanding of Infrastructure/DevSecOps principles and practices.

 Expertise in cloud infrastructure, with knowledge of on-premise systems and migration processes.

 A keen interest in and understanding of enterprise information security.

 High energy, a strong work ethic, and a proactive approach to solving challenges.

 
 What we offer:

 
 Headquartered in NYC with offices in New York, and London, but Remote-first work style; Work anywhere in the US or Canada.

 Meaningful equity plan, giving you a stake in our growth.

 Competitive salary to reward your expertise.

 Private medical care to support your well-being.

 A new laptop and a work-from-home stipend for necessary accessories.

 Unlimited PTO and sick leave to prioritize work-life balance.

 The opportunity to shape our company and disrupt the legal world.

job_title: Member of the Technical Staff - Image / Video Data Engineer
job_description_text:
About the Role

 We are looking for an experienced engineer to lead our large-scale data processing efforts. In this role, you will be responsible for designing, building, and maintaining robust distributed systems that process terabytes of image and video data used to train state-of-the art generative models.

 Key Responsibilities

 
 Design, implement, and optimize complex data processing pipelines responsible for ingesting and transforming large media datasets.

 Manage containerized applications on Kubernetes; deploy and scale distributed systems leveraging Ray to process tasks and orchestrate compute workloads.

 Implement and deploy state-of-the-art ML models for data cleaning, processing, and preparation

 Ensure data quality, diversity, and proper annotation (including captioning) for training readiness

 Work closely in the model development loop to update data as necessitated by the training trajectory

 
 Ideal Experiences

 
 Deep understanding of Python and various file systems for data intensive manipulation and analysis

 Demonstrable experience deploying, managing, and scaling containerized applications on Kubernetes clusters.

 Hands-on experience with distributed computing engines such as Ray, including task scheduling, fault tolerance, and resource management.

 Experience with image and video processing libraries (e.g., OpenCV, FFmpeg)

 Experience working with large image/video datasets, including efficient data handling, transformation, and feature extraction.

 Familiarity with data annotation and captioning processes for ML training datasets

job_title: Senior/Staff ML Researcher - Generative AI Worlds in Brooklyn, NY
job_description_text:
About the Role 

 We’re looking for an exceptional machine learning researcher to join our founding team. You’ll be pushing the frontier of diffusion transformer & world model research, solving core problems of how to create a universal simulator and how to integrate it into customers’ stacks to solve real-world problems.

 Responsibilities: 

 
 Develop state-of-the-art generative models

 Read, implement and improve the latest papers in this field

 Rapid prototyping of ideas, approaches, methods etc.

 
 Ideally you: 

 
 Have 3+ years of machine learning engineering/research experience

 Track record of implementing research papers in PyTorch

 Solid understanding of state-of-the-art generative models (VAEs, GANs, Autoregressive, Diffusion, etc.)

 Knowledge of computer graphics fundamentals (rendering, geometry processing, etc.)

 
 We’re big believers that small, in-person teams are the ones that create the future. We work out of our Brooklyn, New York office, but are flexible with hybrid work schedules. Ideally you are based in NYC.

The role 

 Elayne is hiring a Visual Designer to bring our product and brand to life across every visual touchpoint.

 This is a hands-on design role for a creative generalist who wants real ownership: to define how a new category should look, move, and feel across all static and dynamic content.

 This is not a traditional graphic design role . It is also not a pure product design role or a brand strategy role. You will work across product visuals, website design, motion, marketing materials, print collateral, social assets, decks, and customer-facing education.

 This role will report to Head of Growth and work closely with product and company leadership . Because the role touches both brand and product surfaces, you’ll get to partner closely with the Lead Product Designer on in-product visuals, motion, and interactions.

 What you’ll build 

 As Elayne’s Visual Designer , you will help build the visual system behind a new category of family, legal, and financial software.

 1. A product visual layer that feels clear and trustworthy 

 You will design the visual details that make Elayne easier to use: overlays, cards, empty states, onboarding graphics, icons, illustrations, diagrams, loading states, confirmation moments, and lightweight animations.

 2. Motion and storytelling that bring the product to life 

 You will create short animations, product walkthroughs, web interactions, social clips, and storyboards that make complex workflows easier to grasp.

 3. A web presence that explains the product simply 

 You will design website and landing page visuals that help families, partners, and customers quickly understand what Elayne does and why it matters.

 4. A library of materials the company can use every day 

 You will design flyers, postcards, one-pagers, pitch deck visuals, customer PDFs, email graphics, social assets, event materials, and reusable templates so the team can move faster without redesigning from scratch.

 Location and expectations 

 This is a full-time role based with the team in New York City. 

 We're a small, high-trust team working in a category that's complex, personal, and important. We move quickly, communicate directly, and hold a high bar for the experience we deliver. We care about outcomes more than process. The best work here comes from being close to the details: a confusing user moment, a sales gap, a customer question, a workflow that needs to be clearer. We expect people to pull in the right teammates and turn what they learn into better systems for everyone.

 We expect this person to use AI as leverage: exploring directions faster, prototyping interactions, generating rough concepts, speeding up production. AI should help you move faster, but the final work should still feel careful, intentional, and human.

 What you’ll bring 

 
 
 2–5+ years of experience in brand, visual, web, motion, or digital design

 
 A strong portfolio showing work across web, brand, marketing, and product-adjacent design

 
 Excellent Figma skills and comfort moving quickly from rough concept to finished asset

 
 Strong fundamentals in typography, layout, hierarchy, color, composition, and visual storytelling

 
 Comfort designing marketing and print materials, from one-pagers and decks to event collateral

 
 Strong attention to detail and the ability to keep work consistent across many surfaces

 
 What would make you stand out 

 
 Familiarity with motion and graphics tools like After Effects, Jitter, Lottie, Rive, Illustrator, Photoshop, or Premiere

 Experience with motion, animation, and video: product walkthroughs, short-form social clips, web interactions, or lightweight animated assets

 Comfort designing for real web experiences in Framer or Webflow

 Ability to sketch, storyboard, or prototype ideas quickly before turning them into polished assets

 You use AI as leverage, already using creative and productivity tools to move faster and improve your work

 
 Compensation 

 The expected salary range for this role is $90k to $120k annually, plus equity. This range represents a good-faith estimate for this position at the time of posting. Final compensation will depend on your experience, portfolio, scope, and fit for the role.

 Elayne also offers health insurance and a 401(k) plan.

Started something before?

 This role doesn’t have a predefined job description. If there's a strong fit, we'll work together to find the right place for you, where you can have the biggest impact.

 We're always interested in meeting exceptional people who've built things, whether that was a startup, a product, a community, or something else entirely.

 Apply and tell us what you've built!

About The Role

 We're looking for a Senior Software Engineer to join our engineering team and own major systems end to end. You'll work directly with our CTO and the founding team, meaning you get real ownership: you influence what to build, build it, and ship it.

 Why this role is different

 Estate settlement operates on messy, high-stakes data: legal, financial, and institutional records scattered across dozens of systems that were never built to talk to each other. We’re here to bring peace to families with product that feels accurate and automated.

 You'll build agentic systems and LLM pipelines where correctness truly matters, because the outputs carry real legal and financial weight. And you’ll build thoughtful product where nothing has existed before.

 Turning estate settlement into something a grieving family can actually trust, is a fundamentally human and deeply challenging engineering problem. If you've been craving interesting human-centric product and AI problems with real depth instead of building another SaaS app, this is it. 

 What you'll build

 
 Solving hard problems: agentic asset and inventory processing, LLM-driven extraction with provenance and confidence, and entity resolution across messy, multi-source data. Make AI trustworthy while dealing with legal and financial stakes.

 Integrations with the outside world: banks, government systems, legal entities, and data aggregators, stitched into a single real-time view of a family's estate.

 Brand-new product: some of the most exciting work is still ahead, richer loss support for families, partner interfaces, and plenty more on the roadmap. You'll get to take ideas from zero to shipped and influence what we build next.

 New systems and foundations: the backend and data platforms behind financial assets, document intake, and real-time progress tracking. And end to end efficient systems designed with sound architecture, observability, and the standards that keep us shipping safely with sensitive data.

 
 What we're looking for

 
 5+ years building and shipping production software, strong in Python and/or TypeScript

 Extra points if you're a generalist who likes wearing many hats, or a former founder who knows what it takes to build something from nothing

 Experience with LLM pipelines or AI agents, or the ability and appetite to ramp fast

 A strong bias for correctness and data integrity, especially with sensitive and regulated data

 High-conviction and low-ego: strong opinions held loosely, and you'd rather seek what’s right rather than win the argument

 Comfortable in fintech, legaltech, healthtech, or other similar categories is a bonus, as is experience with Postgres and data engineering

 Real systems-design range: you make sound architecture tradeoffs under ambiguity and own problems end to end

 
 How we work

 We are a small, high-trust team. We care deeply about the people we serve, but we are not precious about process. We move very quickly, communicate directly, and hold a high bar for the quality of the experience we deliver.

 The work here is highly collaborative. The best ideas often come from being close to the details: a family conversation, a product gap, an operational edge case, or a partner need. We expect people to take ownership of outcomes, pull in the right teammates, and turn what they learn into better systems for everyone .

 Location and expectations 

 This is a full-time role based with the rest of the team in New York City .

 This is not a passive 9-to-5 role . We are not looking for performative intensity, but want to be upfront that there will be moments when families, partners, or internal priorities require responsiveness outside typical working hours. The right person is energized by meaningful, high-ownership work and is willing to do what it takes with judgment and care.

 
 Equal opportunity 

 Elayne is an equal opportunity employer. We evaluate qualified applicants based on ability, experience, and potential, and do not discriminate on the basis of any protected characteristic under applicable federal, state, or local laws.

Elayne is hiring a Customer Operations Lead to own the post-loss family experience and harden the operating system behind it.

 This is a senior, hands-on, user-facing operations role for someone who can operate at the intersection of family experience, product operations, service design, legal and financial process mapping, and AI-enabled workflow automation.

 This is not a traditional customer success, account management, or support role. It is also not a back-office CS Ops role. You will work directly with families, understand where the experience is confusing or emotionally difficult, and translate those insights into workflows, service standards, product requirements, escalation paths, and automation opportunities that help Elayne scale.

 This role will report to the CEO and work closely with Growth, Product, Engineering, and external professional partners.

  

 Why this role matters 

 Estate settlement is complicated, but it is not unknowable. Beneath the emotional complexity are patterns, rules, decision points, and recurring moments where users struggle. Elayne’s opportunity is to understand those patterns deeply enough to turn a confusing process into a guided, scalable system.

 This role sits at the center of that work. You will be close enough to users to understand what they actually need, and structured enough to translate those insights into workflows, service standards, escalation paths, product requirements, and automation opportunities.

 The goal is not to solve every case as a one-off. The goal is to identify the branches that matter, systematize the repeatable work, and make sure humans are present for the moments that require judgment, empathy, and trust. Done well, every family we support makes Elayne smarter, more consistent, and more capable of delivering care at scale.

  

 What you’ll build 

 As Elayne’s Customer Operations Lead, you will turn the family experience into an operating model the company can trust, improve, and scale.

 1. A service model families can rely on 

 You will define how Elayne shows up for families across the estate settlement journey: what we explain, when we intervene, how we set expectations, and how we create confidence in moments that are confusing or high-stakes.

 2. A decision architecture for complex workflows 

 You will help break estate settlement into clearer branches, rules, handoffs, and escalation points, so the team is not reinventing the process case by case. The goal is to turn complexity into a system that is understandable for families and usable by the company.

 3. A product feedback loop rooted in user feedback 

 You will translate what happens with families into sharper product priorities: where the experience breaks down, where software should do more, where AI can reduce manual work, and where human touch still matters.

 4. A customer operations function built for leverage 

 Over time, you will build the foundation for the function itself: the standards, training, quality bar, metrics, and operating rhythms that allow Elayne to support more families without scaling headcount linearly or losing trust.

  

 How we work 

 We are a small, high-trust team. We care deeply about the people we serve, but we are not precious about process. We move very quickly, communicate directly, and hold a high bar for the quality of the experience we deliver.

 The work here is highly collaborative. The best ideas often come from being close to the details: a family conversation, a product gap, an operational edge case, or a partner need. We expect people to take ownership of outcomes, pull in the right teammates, and turn what they learn into better systems for everyone .

  

 Location and expectations 

 This is a full-time role based with the rest of the team in New York City .

 This is not a passive 9-to-5 role . We are not looking for performative intensity, but want to be upfront that there will be moments when families, partners, or internal priorities require responsiveness outside typical working hours. The right person is energized by meaningful, high-ownership work and is willing to do what it takes with judgment and care.

  

 What you’ll bring 

 
 
 At least one work experience at a Seed-stage or Series A company : you understand what it means to build in an environment where systems are rapidly evolving, priorities shift quickly, and speed matters more than perfection 


 
 5 to 10 years of experience, in customer operations, product operations, service design, complex case management, services-plus-software, or another high-trust user-facing environment

 
 Strong systems-building ability , with a track record of turning ambiguity into workflows, documentation, operating processes, product requirements, or repeatable service standards

 
 Judgment in sensitive user moments , especially in situations where clarity, empathy, privacy, money, family dynamics, or trust matter.

 
 
  

 What would make you stand out 

 
 
 Ability to learn complex domains quickly , especially legal, financial, operational, or administrative processes that need to become understandable to users, internal teams, and product partners.

 
 Strong cross-functional execution , with the ability to turn frontline user insights into product, engineering, growth, or operating priorities that actually ship.

 
 You use AI as leverage , already using AI tools to move faster and produce better work across messy operational problems.

 
 Experience with complex professional or institutional workflows , especially in categories involving legal, financial, healthcare, insurance, government, or other regulated stakeholders.

 
  

 The person who will thrive 

 
 
 High stamina and high ownership. You understand that early-stage work can be intense, urgent, and underdefined. You do not need perfect instructions to make progress, and when something important needs to get done, you take responsibility for getting to the right outcome.

 
 Highly empathetic, but not soft on structure. You can support people through emotionally difficult moments while still creating the systems, boundaries, and processes required to scale.

 
 Comfortable with ambiguity, but unwilling to leave things ambiguous for others. You can enter an unfamiliar legal, financial, or administrative process, ask the right questions, and turn it into something clear and usable.

 
 Energized by building from the ground floor. You want to be close to users, product, and the operating details that determine whether a company can scale with quality.

 
  

 Compensation 

 The expected salary range for this role is $110 to $180k annually, plus equity. This range represents Elayne’s good-faith estimate for this position at the time of posting. Final compensation will depend on your experience, scope, and fit for the role.

 Elayne also offers health insurance and a 401(k) plan.

  

 Equal opportunity 

 Elayne is an equal opportunity employer. We evaluate qualified applicants based on ability, experience, and potential, and do not discriminate on the basis of any protected characteristic under applicable federal, state, or local laws.

Elevate (YC S24) is hiring high-autonomy, analytical AI Deployment Leads to own end-to-end customer deployments for the next generation of AI-powered acquisitions.

 We’re creating the operating layer for private equity-backed roll-ups and holding companies, turning fragmented data and processes into autonomous, decision-driving systems. As an AI Deployment Lead, you’ll work directly with customers to translate real-world complexity into scalable, agentic software, owning deployments end-to-end while shaping the core platform itself.

 About Elevate

 Elevate is the AI operating system for acquisitions. Our customers are private equity and venture capital-backed roll-ups, holding companies, and serial acquirers. We automate the hardest parts of pre- and post-acquisition growth: system integrations, cross-platform analytics, and end-to-end process automation like accounting month-end close, so operators can focus on building, not wrangling data.

 We’re serving more than 10 mid-market and large-cap customers just one year post-founding, ranging from traditional blue-collar services roll-ups (HVAC, pest control, fire & life safety) to AI-native platforms in white-collar verticals. While we’ve specifically started with rollups as they have structural multi-system data challenges that actively impede taking advantage of the current AI wave, we believe all businesses will ultimately require a context layer that sees across their various operational systems.

 We’re a lean team of 5, and are hiring quickly in response to growing customer demand. Our founding team brings experience across Bain, BCG, Sycamore Partners, Meta, Away, IBM, and Circle . We’re backed by Y Combinator and South Park Commons , and are well-capitalized to hire exceptional people who will accelerate our growth.

 The Role

 AI Deployment Leads are at the core of Elevate’s model. You’ll own the full lifecycle of customer deployments, scoping ambiguous problems, structuring solutions, building and delivering production systems, and ensuring they drive real business outcomes.

 You’ll sit at the intersection of customer and product engineering , acting as the primary interface with operators while collaborating closely with our internal team to extend the platform. You’ll report directly to the C-suite and have visibility into every part of the business.

 This role is designed for strong analytical leaders from consulting, finance, or internal ops who want to build . You’ll take ambiguous, real-world business problems and turn them into structured, executable AI systems. What matters most is ownership, intensity, and the ability to move from problem to production quickly. You will define the agentic system that will end up running thousands of businesses across the real economy.

 What You’ll Do

 
 
 Own customer deployments end-to-end — scoping, business logic translation, data ingestion, pipeline delivery, and ongoing optimization — working directly with mid-market to large-cap operators

 Act as the primary point of contact for customers , working directly with operators, finance teams, and executives to drive timelines, unblock work, and ensure deployments are delivered quickly and effectively

 
 Translate ambiguous business problems into structured data models, workflows, and agentic systems

 
 Build and extend our agentic AI platform , improving Elevate’s core platform based on real customer needs in direct collaboration with our engineering team

 
 Support go-to-market by joining sales calls and identifying customer expansion opportunities

 
 Who You Are

 
 2+ years experience in consulting or finance, with a desire to grow your technical chops

 Highly analytical, with experience working with messy data (SQL, Excel, or similar tools)

 Strong communication with business intuition - you’re comfortable working with executives and operators

 Scrappy, autonomous, and action-oriented

 Hungry to build

 
 Bonus Points

 
 Have hands-on experience building with AI tools and LLM APIs, even for personal use

 SQL and Python proficiency or a strong desire to get there quickly

 Experience with data pipelines, system integrations, or internal tooling

 Exposure to private equity, roll-ups, or M&A

 
 Our Values

 Bias to Action : Ship something 80% right and learn from customers. We value momentum over perfection.

 Assume Good Intentions : We move fast across disciplines—founders, engineers, customers in the same threads. We solve problems together, not through blame.

 Professional Development : Everyone is building the next version of themselves. We commit time and energy to making each other better every day.

 Compensation

 Competitive cash compensation and meaningful equity in an early-stage, high-growth company

Ellipsis is an AI developer tool that automatically reviews code and fixes bugs, and is used by hundreds of companies.

 Our mission is to build an AI software engineer. The Ellipsis platform already includes:

 
 
 Automatic PR reviews : our GitHub app helps teams merge code ~13% faster by catching logical bugs, style guide violations, security issues, anti-patterns, and more.

 
 Interactive dialogue : Ellipsis can respond to PR comments and Slack messages to answer questions and make code changes.

 
 Agentic codebase search : we use multi-step hybrid vector and keyword search to answer hard queries.

 
 Asynchronous code generation : we built a sandbox that allows our agent to build, lint, and test the code it generates - outside the IDE, so developers can parallelize.

 
 Customizable workflows : users can automate tasks like standup updates and code change notifications.

 
 We believe the world is at an inflection point where LLMs are finally becoming useful for async code generation for real, complex features. The productivity of human engineers will no longer be limited by their typing speed, but their ability to manage a fleet of AI engineering agents.

 We hope you’ll help us build the future.

 Requirements

 You should apply if:

 
 You’re excited about defining the future of software engineering

 You have experience building LLM agents

 You’ve tried out our product and browsed the docs

 You want significant product ownership

 You’re interested in wearing many hats across all levels of the stack

 You’re excited to be the first hire and work directly with the founders, Hunter and Nick

 
 You should not apply if:

 
 You’re uninterested in working long hours on hard problems

 You’re unwilling to work in-person in NYC (our office is in Union Square)

 
 Some technologies you’ll use:

 
 Agents: OpenAI, Claude, PromptLayer, and our homemade agents/evals framework

 Backend: Python, FastAPI, Hatchet, Postgres, DynamoDB, Turbopuffer, Docker, Modal, AWS

 Frontend: TypeScript, Next.js, Vercel, Tailwind

 …and any others you see fit. The above are just what we use today - you’ll be able to choose what tools you use to get the job done.

 
 For more about Ellipsis, check out our docs ( https://ellipsis.dev/docs ) and blog ( https://ellipsis.dev/blog ).

About Empirical Health 

 We're building the future of primary care. 99% of your health is defined outside the doctor’s office. Empirical helps you manage it.

 Empirical Health is a virtual-first medical service that uses artificial intelligence and health sensors on wearable devices (Apple Watch, Fitbit, Google Pixel, Samsung watch and more) to scale proactive healthcare to everyone. We're building a product that gives every user an AI-powered personal health assistant, backed by a real human doctor who can provide actual diagnosis, treatment, and care. 
 
In this role you will work with our doctors and interact with real patients to build the tools needed to keep them healthy. Do you want to use your software engineering and design skills to save lives and make healthcare better for everyone? At Empirical Health, we're inventing the future of primary care – and we want you.

 About the role 

 From blueprint to breakthrough, Design Engineers are responsible for the core of Empirical Health: our patient-facing mobile app.

 Some examples of what you might do:

 
 Craft an intuitive data visualization to help our patients understand their overall health score (based on a formula created by a doctor), how their health is changing, and what actions have the highest impact in improving their health.

 Design GenAI UI patterns that go beyond chat.

 Launch new features for our mobile app to help improve our AI generated care plans for patients with different conditions such as POTS, hypertension, or sleep apnea.

 Design and build flows that help patients understand tradeoffs in cost and quality between different options (for example, an in-lab sleep apnea testing can cost $3,000 vs $200 for an at-home test).

 
 We expect design engineers to push code to production in the first week and complete a major project that improves the lives of real patients by the end of the first 1-2 months. 
 
This job is in person 5 days a week in New York City (Union Square in Manhattan), but hybrid/remote days can be arranged when necessary.

 About you 

 We’re looking for someone with strong UI engineering skills and an obsession with design to ship real features for our users.

 Ideally, you have:

 
 Strong knowledge of TypeScript or JavaScript and React or React Native.

 A strong desire to make an impact on the healthcare system

 A high level of skill in interaction design, usability, and data visualization.

 Excellence in the basics: simple code, reasonable automated tests, code reviews, clear comments, and quick iteration cycles.

 Strong owner's mindset.

 
 Nice to haves:

 
 Experience with mobile interface design (e.g., iOS Human Interface Guidelines).

 Exposure to healthcare, bioinformatics, or digital health is helpful, but if you're willing to learn, we're willing to teach.

 Visual design or motion design.

 
 Technologies we work with 

 
 React Native (mobile app), Node.js (server), and TypeScript (both)

 PostgreSQL (database)

 Swift and Kotlin (native modules in iOS and Android)

 
 Why work with us 

 
 
 We ship —we don’t do year-long projects that are outdated before they’re even finished. We ship incrementally, so that we’re constantly testing whether our product direction resonates with real users.

 
 Our work has deep impact . We directly help people–often with serious illnesses like Long Covid or heart disease–live healthier lives. We’re focused on real patient care, not the back office.

 
 We’re small, on purpose. We’re a team of 4 people, even though we’ve raised enough capital to hire more. We believe a small team with high agency and skill can accomplish great things.

 
 Experienced founding team . Our founders include a UC San Francisco Assistant Professor and a serial entrepreneur who scaled their previous startup to 10 million users.

 
 Wear multiple hats. We believe the boundaries between design, product, and engineering are porous and flexible, and we embrace people who are interdisciplinary.

The Role: 

 We are seeking a hands‑on Frontend Tech Lead who thrives in building from 0‑1 and driving execution, directly contributing production‑level code while shaping our core technical foundation. You will collaborate closely with the founding team to architect, develop, and scale F2’s AI‑driven enterprise UI platform.

 What you’ll do: 

 
 
 Hands‑on UI system building. Architect and implement foundational frontend infrastructure and features, actively coding and delivering critical React/TypeScript components from scratch.



 
 Own the frontend architecture. Define and execute the technical vision and multi‑year roadmap for our React‑based UI platform, ensuring rapid iteration and effective scaling of user‑facing experiences.



 
 Design for performance, security, and accessibility. Build high‑performance, responsive interfaces that meet SOC 2 and accessibility requirements, with a focus on maintainability and cost‑efficiency.



 
 Integrate AI at the UI layer. Collaborate with our team to integrate intelligent agents, data visualizations, and interactive tools that surface AI‑driven insights within the application.



 
 Establish frontend engineering excellence. Set and uphold coding standards, component library conventions, and review processes; mentor engineers to foster a culture of reliable, test‑driven frontend development.



 
 Cross‑functional collaboration. Partner with product, design, backend, and enterprise customers to translate requirements into elegant UI solutions and deliverables.



 
 What you bring: 

 
 
 Hands‑on technical leadership. 7+ years building and scaling mission‑critical frontend systems in TypeScript and React, with demonstrated experience architecting from the ground up.



 
 Practical API design expertise. Deep experience designing and consuming RESTful and GraphQL APIs, including state management and efficient data‑fetching strategies.



 
 Execution‑driven mindset. You thrive in environments where you own features end‑to‑end, iterate rapidly, and deliver production‑ready UI solutions.



 
 Familiarity with CSS‑in‑JS (e.g. Styled Components, Emotion) or utility‑first frameworks (e.g. Tailwind), as well as Next.js, Webpack, and frontend performance profiling.



 
 Passionate builder who enjoys solving complex UI challenges and iterating toward clear, impactful goals.

What you'll do in the role: 

 
 
 Build our Product. Own and implement software features that help to power our AI platform helping private markets investors analyze deals with velocity and accuracy. In this role you will be actively coding and delivering critical platform components from scratch.

 
 Deliver excellence and quality. Set and uphold coding standards, architectural patterns, and review processes; mentor engineers. Co-own our culture of practical, reliable, and test-driven development that rapidly addresses business needs.

 
 Build for Scale. Contribute to monitoring & scaling the architecture of the platform and the infrastructure.

 
 Cross-functional collaboration. Actively partner with product, security, and go-to-market teams to translate our roadmap into features and deliverables.

 
 What you bring to the role: 

 
 4+ years of fullstack software engineering experience.

 Passion for engineering and learning.

 AI fluency, and ability to build with AI at the core

 Knowledgeable about modern web application development practices.

 Familiarity with Python, Fast API, TypeScript, Node.js, Supabase, AWS is a plus.

The Role:

 As AI Deployment Strategist , you’ll work across customer success, sales, and engineering to ensure technical clarity and execution—before, during, and after the sale of our cutting-edge F2 product. You’ll become the AI prompting authority for the business, forming the technical backbone of the go-to-market motion.

 You will: 

 
 
 Support the F2 sales process by joining prospect and client calls to address technical product questions



 
 Own customer due diligence : complete InfoSec questionnaires, and continuously improve our DDQ knowledge base



 
 Audit reports generated by users, flag issues to engineering, and support customers in getting value from our AI



 
 Prompt engineering : create natural language prompts tailored to customer needs to ensure accurate, relevant report output



 
 Deliver light technical implementations , such as configuring SSO or provisioning custom data retention policies



 
 Ensure CRM hygiene : maintain clean, structured data to help the customer success and sales teams scale effectively



 
 Be the voice of the customer to the engineering team— providing real feedback that helps improve the product



 
 Who You Are 

 You’re a versatile operator who can translate between engineers and customers. You know how to handle enterprise buyers, explain complex systems clearly, and work cross-functionally to deliver high-impact outcomes.

 
 
 Experience prompting and working with LLMs and AI SaaS tools



 
 Familiarity with enterprise information security concepts and due diligence processes



 
 Strong communicator who is commercially minded , customer-obsessed, and tech fluent. You can explain complex concepts to non technical audiences



 
 Ability to project manage complex workflows and technical deliverables without writing production code



 
 Strong on keyboard skills, technically sharp, and eager to find a path to leveling up your coding skills



 
 Based in NYC — we believe the best work is done together, in person at our beautiful corporate HQ in NYC. You’ll have flexibility to work remotely at least 1 day per week.



 
 Nice to Haves 

 
 
 Prior experience reviewing or writing investment memos or working as an investment analyst is a big plus



 
 Previous roles in technical sales, solutions engineering, customer success, or AI implementation



 
 Familiarity with reporting tools, natural language outputs, or unstructured document processing

job_description_text:
**note that this role is for an international, remote hire 

 Fiber AI is a comprehensive data provider with the freshest data on 40M+ companies, 850M+ people, and 30M+ jobs. We’ve raised >$5M, generate >$1.5M ARR, and are expanding our mid-market enterprise contracts.

 What will you work on? 

 We're hiring a Founding Chief of Staff / Marketing Strategy & Operations Lead to own day-to-day execution and drive new ideas across our products. This role is for someone who comes up with the creative, ships it, reads the numbers, and scales what works. We want a self-starter, not someone who waits for a detailed brief of instructions to follow. Flawless English is a must for this role. 

 This is a fully remote role and we're hiring internationally, so you can be based anywhere in the world. It's 40 hours a week of work total, but it's not a 9am-to-5pm job. We move fast and across time zones, so the schedule flexes: expect to shift hours to overlap with the team, and to put in a couple of hours on weekends to maintain business operations. If you want a rigid clock-out-at-5pm routine, this isn't the role. If you like real ownership and don't mind the work shaping your hours, you'll thrive here.

 Core Responsibilities: 

 
 
 Growth & creative : you generate new marketing angles, ad creatives, and channel experiments, then run them. You think in terms of what will actually move revenue, and you're hungry to scale. This is the core of the role.

 
 Marketing copy : landing pages, emails, ads, social. The copy that we write must sound natural, casual, & like a real person wrote it.

 
 AI tooling & UGC : you pick up new AI tools fast and ship with them. We will be building AI harnesses to automate finding signals and outbound.

 
 Vibe coding : you use AI tools (Cursor, Claude Code, v0, Replit) to build small things yourself: automations, internal tools, site tweaks.

 
 Support & site : own the customer inbox; answer customer questions on Slack and help with technical debugging.

 
 You're a fit if: you've actually grown something before and can point to it; you write excellent, native-sounding English; you're technical-adjacent and unafraid of code; and you move fast without hand-holding.

 Comp: $3,000/month guaranteed base, plus a revenue-tied bonus paid out monthly which is uncapped . The more you grow the business, the more you make. Strong performers should be clearing $5-6k/month; if you're exceptional and the numbers reflect it, there's no ceiling on that.

About FINNY 

 FINNY is a growth platform for financial advisors. We are on a mission to make great financial advice easier to find. Today, access to quality financial guidance is limited, not because advisors don’t exist, but because the right connections are hard to make when it matters. We’re fixing that with AI-powered tools that help advisors find, engage, and retain the clients they can genuinely help.

 We’ve raised a 4.3 million pre-seed from Y-Combinator in S24 and now $17M Series A led by Venrock. We work with firms across the wealth management ecosystem, and have been recognized as a leader in fintech innovation—winning #1 at the Morningstar Fintech Showcase and being featured across the industry.

 We’re based in Chelsea, NYC, building fast and ambitious systems at the intersection of data, AI, and real-world wealth services.

 About the Team 

 Being a Machine Learning Engineer at FINNY means owning the models that power search, matching, ranking, recommendations, data and intelligent automation across the product. This is a model first role. While you’ll work with real production data and pipelines, your primary impact comes from designing, training, evaluating, and improving ML systems that directly shape user outcomes. You’ll partner closely with product, backend, and frontend teams to turn ambiguous problems into measurable model improvements.

 What You’ll Do 

 Help build FINNY’s core models 

 
 Design, train, and iterate on custom models that power data imputation, prospect and audience recommendations, campaign customization and personalization, and automations.

 
 Build & improve models in production 

 
 Take models from research → experimentation → deployment → iteration

 Own offline evaluation, online metrics, and feedback loops

 Improve model performance over time through better objectives, features, and training strategies—not just more data

 
 Advanced modeling & experimentation 

 
 Apply and adapt techniques such as:

 Operate effectively in low-signal, noisy, or cold-start environments 


 
 Contribute to ML systems & infrastructure 

 
 Work with backend engineers to productionize models reliably and at scale

 Help define standards for model versioning, evaluation, deployment, and monitoring

 Influence long-term ML strategy and reduce technical debt in modeling workflows

 
 What We’re Looking For 

 You’re a model builder at heart 

 
 You care deeply about how models learn, not just how pipelines run

 You’re comfortable reasoning about loss functions, tradeoffs, and evaluation

 You enjoy designing solutions when the problem is underspecified and data is imperfect

 
 You’re strong technically 

 
 Very strong Python with extensive hands-on experience building ML systems.

 Strong statistical and mathematical foundations.

 Proven experience training, fine-tuning, and deploying custom models into production, not just experimentation or offline research

 Experience designing loss functions, evaluation metrics, and validation strategies aligned with real-world product objectives

 Familiarity with model lifecycle management: versioning, reproducibility, monitoring, and iteration in production environments

 
 You’ve shipped ML systems before 

 
 You’ve taken models beyond notebooks and into real products

 You understand failure modes, monitoring, and iteration in production ML

 Startup experience

 
 Your working style 

 
 You tackle ambiguity head-on and turn fuzzy problems into concrete experiments

 You move fast, iterate, and aren’t precious about first approaches

 You communicate clearly about model behavior, limitations, and tradeoffs

 In-person, NYC (5 days/week in Chelsea office)

 
 Compensation & Benefits 

 FINNY offers a competitive compensation package including:

 
 Competitive salary and equity

 Medical, dental, and vision insurance

 Flexible paid time off

 401(k)

 Food and meals provided in our NYC office

 Team offsites and events

 
 Equal Opportunity Employer 

 FINNY is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to diversity and inclusion in our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

About Finosu 

 Finosu is an AI-native loan servicer. What does that mean? Well, a servicer handles the communications and payment rails for lenders, think of it as customer support for lenders.

 Where does the AI-native piece come in?

 Usually, all that communication is staffed by thousands of humans in remote place like the Philippines, India and other English-speaking countries where people are doing arbitrage on cheap English speakers (one co-founder grew up near a couple). Now let’s remove all the humans doing that. Not just from the front-office, but the back office as well. At Finosu, we believe superior customer support comes from removing humans when there is no more bottlenecks on business hours, and everything is compliant default.

 Things to Know 

 We are a start-up and our processes are in flux. Priorities might shift more abruptly than at a more establish company.

 
 We’re in office, we have found that collaboration especially in a context-rich field is important
 
 If you HAVE to be remote to start, we can work on a plan

 


 You will own and be intimate with a high number of things. This will allow you to go deep and truly be the point person for parts of the company. While painful, growth will come from it.

 
 About the Role 

 This is a infra/product role to start. However, as we scale, it will be more focus on infra. The reason we think it’s good to start 50-50 is because we think that sometimes the best thing to do from an infra perspective involves having a good idea of the business requirements and potentially rearchitecting the product.

 In this role

 
 Scale our AI agents. Figure out to bypass vendors caps whether by clever orchestration or moving to self-hosting when relevant

 Identify needless spend from SaaS bloat and cut off unneeded vendors

 Implement an observability stack so we can understand how each component of the system is doing

 
 Projects people in this role have taken or will take:

 
 Migrate from Datadog to Grafana. Dear lord, that saved us so much money, and we would love to do that again in other places

 K8 optimization. Figure out the optimal number of servers and dynamically scale off certain metrics since CPU and Memory aren’t the bottlenecks

 Figure out why Postgres prepared statements are causing a memory leak.

 
 About You

 
 While we do have a roadmap, we expect you to contribute and push back if you feel there are more important projects to work you. We love tension and conflict if it creates a better product

 An ability to learn fast, we don’t care about experience as much as slope. The world is changing underneath us, 2 years ago it would be weird if an engineer locked himself in a room and talked to the computer to make software, but here we are…

 Love observability. A lot of our need comes down to understanding why things happen, so observability is important

 
 About You

 Product minded, do you think the product manager discipline is outdated and engineers should be leading these decisions? While we refuse to comment on things that might get us in trouble in the future, we love engineers who want to make product decisions. In this role, you will be the product manager of the projects you own

job_description_text:
Office rent is the second largest expense item on a company’s P&L, with over $700B spent annually. The role of the corporate real estate (CRE) function within an organization has evolved beyond a pure cost center into a true value driver. Business leaders now demand a new level of operational and data maturity in response to complex workplace patterns to maximize workplace effectiveness, address carbon neutrality, and optimize overall spend. This has only intensified as AI establishes a cornerstone role in all support functions.

 FlexDesk is building the product suite to support this digital transformation and equip businesses with a data intelligence and workflow platform to maximize the ROI on their real estate.

 Overview 

 FlexDesk is recruiting a founding sales development representative to join its team. This role will report directly to the CEO and work alongside a peer on the sales team to help drive the growth of a fast-growing startup.

 You’ll be at the forefront of establishing new relationships with Corporate Real Estate and Workplace Experience leaders. You will be responsible for executing upon and refining our cold outbound lead generation playbook.

 As a successful SDR, you must be a self-starter who is comfortable working independently as well as part of a team. You are tenacious, organized, cross-functional, and can quickly build rapport with prospective customers. You will be at the tip of the spear to FlexDesk’s growth, and have uncapped growth opportunities as we define the sales culture and expand the team.

 Responsibilities 

 
 Become an expert in our target persona, their problems/objectives, and how that maps to the FlexDesk value proposition

 Possess a hunter mentality, with the ability to build pipeline through list-building, cold outreach (email, phone, LinkedIn), in-person networking, and account-based marketing

 Use an organized approach to develop and experiment with different outbound campaigns and scripts

 Navigate complex organizational structures and use creative tactics to reach target personas within prospective customers

 Identify best practices to refine the sales development playbook and maintain reporting metrics

 Represent the FlexDesk brand by exuding a professional presence, establishing industry rapport, and generating visibility on key social media channels

 Serve as an internal force-multiplier, culture-carrier, and standard for velocity

 Create a feedback loop for translating front-line conversations with prospects into additional product needs

 Manage your career progression as you evolve into a closing role

 
 Qualifications 

 
 2+ years experience in a SaaS role selling to mid-market and enterprise customers

 Track record of consistently exceeding quota

 Strong work ethic and team-first mindset where everyone is excited to chip in beyond their defined responsibilities

 Nose for value and ability to prioritize time across competing initiatives

 SF/NYC hybrid preferred, remote considered

 Bonus points
 
 Experience selling to a shared services function like HR, Finance, Corporate Travel, Procurement, or CRE

 Experience at a multi-product “compound” startup

 


 
 What We Offer 

 
 A strong product suite that customers love

 Ground-floor role at a YC-backed startup

 Steep career progression and growth opportunities

 Offsite company trips

 Competitive compensation

 Healthcare benefits and 401K + 3% match

Full-time | In-Person – New York City

 Compensation: $115K OTE (approx. $75K base + $40K variable, tied to qualified demos booked) 

 About Us 

 Founded in 2023, Flint is an AI platform built for schools. In two years we've partnered with 250+ schools across the U.S. and abroad.

 You're joining at a real inflection point. Some of our GTM playbooks already work. Others we're still writing. Your job is to lean hard on the ones that work, help us pressure-test the ones that don't, and turn high-potential leads into demos on the calendar.

 Job Description 

 The goal is simple to state and hard to do: book qualified demos.

 Our GTM engineering team builds and enriches the lead list, and most of what reaches you is already warm: either inbound interest, or outbound prospects the team has run through a sequence and warmed up. You take those leads and turn them into meetings with the people who can actually say yes. Mostly that's the phone, and we won't pretend otherwise, but we don't care whether your sharpest tool is calling, email, LinkedIn, or something we haven't tried yet. We care that good leads turn into real conversations, and that school leaders come away glad they picked up.

 What you’ll do

 
 Turn leads from our GTM engineering team into qualified demos with school leaders and decision-makers.

 Run real outreach (phone, email, LinkedIn, whatever earns a reply) and follow up like it matters, because it does.

 Work out which messages land and which personas convert, then tell us, so the playbook gets sharper every week.

 Represent Flint in early conversations the way that makes a school want a second one.

 
 Career Growth & Trajectory

 This role is a launchpad, on purpose.

 For now, we need someone who can convert leads into qualified demos, full stop. But prove you can book them and want to learn to close them? Go for it. Find yourself more drawn to GTM engineering, growth systems, or sales ops? We'll back that too.

 Narrow goal, wide-open horizon. Book the demos first. After that, the GTM world is your oyster.

 What we're looking for 

 
 You're relentless and rejection doesn't rattle you. This job has a lot of "no" before the "yes."

 You're comfortable living on the phone.

 You communicate clearly and directly, and you can read a room.

 You'd rather figure out what works than run someone else's script.

 You've got the judgment to represent Flint well to school leaders, and you're driven by outcomes, not activity.

 
 Nice to have, but not required

 
 B2B software sales experience.

 Familiarity with HubSpot, Apollo, or similar tools.

 Previous EdTech experience.

 Experience selling to schools or public-sector buyers.

 
 None of these are dealbreakers. We've seen great BDRs come from stranger places.

 What we offer 

 
 Meaningful equity. You're early, and we mean it.

 Health, dental, and vision insurance.

 Personal device stipend.

 A front-row seat at an early-stage company reshaping how AI shows up in schools.

 
 How to Apply 

 Questions or want to know more? Apply here: https://app.dover.com/apply/flintk12/f36a18ae-68a7-41a5-a3cb-10d0267ed14e 

 We'd like you to treat this listing as a deal to close. Think you're the right person? Don't tell us. Close us on it.
