 You’ll create and own a portfolio of channel partner relationships end-to-end, sourcing new ones, building further relationships with the ones we have and turning both into consistent, high-quality pipeline generation. The function is still being built from the ground up, so you’ll also help shape how we engage partners, and where we invest now and in the future.

 This role is part of the Sales team and works daily with our Revenue, Marketing, and EPD teams.

 What You’ll Own 

 
 Own quarterly revenue targets for the channel. Scope, build, and manage a portfolio of partner relationships with the goal of engaging net-new partners each quarter and increasing deal flow consistency from existing ones.

 Drive step-change growth in the partner channel by identifying opportunities to scale efficiently, including leveraging AI and automation to increase output and improve performance.

 Lead partner engagement through product education, market context, and co-developing how partners identify and position high-quality opportunities.

 Maintain end-to-end visibility on partner-sourced deals, proactively identifying risks and stepping in at key moments to improve conversion outcomes.

 Use data to guide investment decisions. Spot what’s working, what’s not, and adjust accordingly.

 Translate partner and market feedback into actionable product direction, helping shape roadmap priorities and improve product-market fit.

 Represent Hadrius within your partner ecosystem, building credibility through industry events, forums, and partner-led environments.

 
 Who You Are 

 
 3–6 years in partnerships, business development, sales, consulting, fintech, B2B SaaS, or related operator roles.

 Commercially driven and comfortable owning hard numbers. You treat pipeline and revenue targets as personal commitments, not guidance.

 A high-touch relationship builder who understands the patience that partnerships require and the discipline needed to stay top of mind across a broad portfolio.

 Tech-savvy and product-curious. You can quickly translate complex product capabilities into clear, audience-specific value propositions.

 AI-forward and always looking for ways to use new tools to work smarter, move faster, and increase impact.

 A strategic thinker who can map complex partner ecosystems to clear, executable plans.

 Highly collaborative, low ego, and coachable. You thrive in cross-functional environments and have strong stakeholder management instincts.

 A builder at heart who is excited to help shape and grow a function at a fast-moving company.

 
 Bonus: 

 
 Proven track record in a quota-carrying or commercially accountable role, ideally with responsibility for pipeline generation, partner activation, or revenue contribution.

 Experience in high-growth or early-stage B2B SaaS or fintech

 Background in financial services, wealth management, or RIA-adjacent ecosystems (custodians, wealthtech, broker-dealers, asset managers)

 Understanding of compliance, regulatory operations, or finance systems is a plus.

 
 Benefits 

 
 
 401(k): 100% match up to 6%

 
 Living Stipend: living stipend if south of Chambers Street ($18,000 annually)

 
 Insurance: Healthcare, dental, vision, etc.

 Unlimited PTO: 

 Dinner Credit

Job Description: Customer Operations & Support

 About Hadrius

 Hadrius automates and consolidates securities compliance for investment firms. To do this, we’re building the automated back office for financial firms, using AI to analyze millions of data points from emails to text messages, marketing copy, investment accounts, filings, and more to identify regulatory violations.

 Today, over 300+ financial institutions managing $4T+ AUM rely on Hadrius to automate their regulatory compliance. We’re the fastest growing company in the space and the emerging category leader, going from our launch out of the YC W23 batch to $XMs+ ARR in under 24 months and haven’t slowed down since. We have a world-class team and product competing against legacy software that hasn't innovated in decades, and are looking for exceptional people dedicated to our shared mission to join.

 We're scaling fast, and we're building the most leveraged Customer Success motion in the industry — one where data, automation, and focused human touch create outsized outcomes for hundreds of firms at once.

 The Role

 This is a foundational hire. As Hadrius's first dedicated Customer Operations & Support teammate, you'll own the operational backbone of our Customer Success function — protecting our Account Managers' focus, tracking customer insight/requests alongside their current status, and building the systems and automations that let our CS team scale.

 This role sits at the intersection of support, operations, and insights . You'll start by reducing ticket load across the team, then progressively take ownership of the automation and infrastructure our Forward-Deployed Engineers are building — becoming the durable owner of CS operations as we grow.

 This role reports to the Manager of Customer Success and works daily with our FDEs, Scaled CSM, and the full AM team. You and the Scaled CSM are a true pair. They run the customer-facing SMB motion, you build and maintain the infrastructure that makes it scale.

 What You'll Own

 Near-term (first 30 days): Support + Insights 

 
 Own the tier-1 ticket queue across all segments. Resolve, triage, and escalate.

 Systematically capture and tag customer insights from tickets, AI Notetaker, and Customer CRM. Weekly synthesis to the Manager of Customer Success and bi-weekly theme reports to EPD.

 Maintain and expand our FAQ library and routing logic configuration to drive ticket deflection.

 Treat automation as a discipline: identify high-frequency manual work across CS and design automations that eliminate it. The goal is a continuously declining of manual tickets-per-account ratio.

 Project management and triage for the broader CS team.

 
 Progressive (months 1-3): CS Operations 

 
 Inherit CS operational infrastructure from our FDEs as their quarterly engagement completes.

 Maintain and evolve the customer health scoring model and reporting infrastructure.

 
 Ongoing: 

 
 Ticket-volume reduction: partner with Scaled CSMs and EPD to reduce tickets-per-account by driving self-service, automation, and product improvements.

 
 Who You Are

 
 
 3–5 years of experience in Customer Success Operations, Support, or Customer Operations roles at a B2B SaaS company.

 
 Product minded. You're comfortable resolving tickets and playing with the platform to answer tickets.

 
 Systems thinker. You see repeat tickets as a signal, not a task. You ask "what's the root cause and how do we kill the whole category?"

 
 Inquisitive. You genuinely love playing with product and trying new tech.

 
 Data-fluent. You can build reports and work with product telemetry.

 
 Tooling depth: HubSpot admin experience is strongly preferred; familiarity with Pylon, Outreach, and CS-adjacent tools (Gainsight/Catalyst/Vitally, even if not implementing) is a plus.

 
 Comfort with ambiguity. You seize opportunity and are excited to build.

 
 Success Looks Like

 First 7 days: 

 
 Ramp on our ICP, value proposition, Hadrius platform, pricing/packaging, and roadmap

 Pass 3 of our product certifications

 Become familiarized with our systems and tooling

 Map out any dependencies, hand offs, and processes — and where they break

 Build a deep enough mental model in week 1 that by week 2, you have informed opinions to test

 Report your first bug/feature request

 
 First 14 days: 

 
 Become an expert on our product — and where/how to find answers to niche questions

 Answer your first 30 customer tickets (with heavy help)

 Identifying at least 1 surface area where we can score a win through automation

 Mapped out ticket tiering, tagging, and pipelines — with minor improvements shipped

 
 At 45 days: 

 
 Tier-1 ticket SLA (first response <2 hours) held across the queue

 Weekly insight synthesis established as a reliable cadence

 Ticket deflection rate improving month-over-month

 AMs report meaningful reduction in ticket work

 If applicable, a new version of Customer Health Score shipped

 
 At 90 days: 

 
 Full ownership of CS operational tooling (HubSpot, Pylon, Outreach, reporting)

 Health scoring infrastructure maintained independently of FDEs

 Handoff from FDEs complete — CS operates on a durable self-owned stack

 CS operates as a fully instrumented function — every AM has real-time visibility into their book's health, expansion, and risk

 Tickets-per-AM metric down 40%+ from baseline

 Escalation triggers are running automatically

 
 Compensation

 Total Target Compensation (OTE): $110,000–$135,000 

 
 
 Base salary: $100,000–$120,000

 
 Variable compensation: $10,000–$15,000

 Equity 

 
 Benefits: full medical/dental/vision, 401(k), unlimited PTO

 
 Benefits 

 
 
 401k: 100% match up to 6%

 
 Living Stipend: living stipend if below Chambers street ( 18,000 Annually) 


 
 Insurance: Healthcare, dental, vision, etc.

 Unlimited PTO 

 
 Range reflects experience level and technical depth. Higher end of range for candidates with HubSpot admin certification, direct CS Ops experience at a scaling B2B SaaS, or financial services background.

 You’ll work directly with the founding team to define our go-to-market strategy, refine our motion across segments, and build the systems and people to sustain rapid expansion.  About You

 Qualifications

 We're hiring a PM to join a small, super-high-velocity product team. You'll own meaningful surface area from day one across a product that's reshaping compliance, and you’ll be part of a team that works incredibly hard and is seeking other exceptional people excited to do the same.

This is a full-time role as a Product Marketing Lead, located onsite in New York, NY. 

 We’re looking for a sharp, execution-driven Product Marketing Lead to define our ICP, positioning and messaging, sales enablement, and product launches, and turn that into measurable revenue impact. This is a foundational GTM role: you will directly influence pipeline, win rates, and sales velocity.

 You’ll sit at the center of Sales, Product, and Marketing, ensuring we are telling a clear, differentiated story in every deal, every campaign, and every product launch. You’ll own how the market understands Hadrius and why we win.

 This is not a “support” function, this is a revenue-driving role. You will operate as a mini Head of PMM, owning strategy and execution end-to-end.

 You will work directly with the founding team to design and build the future of the financial compliance system. We pride ourselves on having a culture that rewards impact and success generously.  What “Good” Looks Like

The property management industry is ripe for disruption, and the perfect vertical for natural language automation. 

 Haven is building an autonomous workforce for the property management industry. Our suite of AI agents connect to property data and integrate with a property manager’s software to automate manual, rote tasks on top of the systems that they already use today. 

 Our AI agents are already live across tens of thousands of residential properties, and we’re on track to double before the end of the year.

 We’re backed by top VCs such as YCombinator, and notable angels like the founder of Salesloft. 

 Our Co-founders previously built and sold a vertical SaaS company in less than 2 years, disrupting another ossified industry (K-12 admissions).

 The Problem We're Solving: 

 The US rental industry generates $1+ Trillion per year, most of which is administered by property management companies that run on archaic Property Management Systems (PMS). These Legacy PMS generate Billions in revenue, yet they remain slow, incredibly complicated to use, and painfully manual.

 PMSs automate only a small part of a property manager’s work, forcing companies to scale by hiring additional, often less experienced staff for labor-intensive tasks. This approach adds inefficiency and cost without truly solving the underlying problem. As a result, the property management industry suffers from razor-thin margins, a 70% employee turnover rate, and difficulty scaling efficiently.

 Our Solution: 

 We’re leveraging the recent advancements in LLMs to automate the industry’s most arduous manual workflows — and wedging ourselves into one of the largest vertical SaaS opportunities that exist today.

 Our first autonomous worker is automating the entire maintenance coordination process. This means answering the phone when tenants have a maintenance issue, diagnosing the issue, dispatching a technician, and then following up with tenants to make sure the issue was resolved. This means property managers can scale their operations without growing headcount, while providing better, faster service to their tenants.

 Your Role: 

 You'll work closely with the founders to discover, spec, and build automations that transform how property management companies operate. As a founding engineer, you'll tackle fascinating technical challenges at the intersection of LLMs and legacy software systems, helping us create truly autonomous workers that can handle complex human workflows.

 
 Complex Architectural Puzzles: Navigate challenges in natural language interfaces, model evaluation complexities, customizing models to diverse client-specific needs, and multi-modal delivery (i.e., SMS, Voice, and embedded chat widget).

 Domain-Specific Models: Fine-tune and evaluate models to handle the nuanced requirements of property management, including maintenance diagnosis, vendor coordination, and tenant communication.

 Model Evaluations and Algorithms: Conceptualize and implement multi-step conversational workflows, retrieval augmented generation (RAG), search, and QA algorithms.

 Engineering Large-scale LLM Systems: Ensure optimal performance while engineering and assessing vast interconnected LLM systems.

 Data Harvesting and Structuring: Design and build pipelines that can ingest, structure, and retrieve diverse property management data to augment our LLMs with domain knowledge.

 High-Performance Infrastructure: Optimize for quick response times and efficient model inference while maintaining reliability at scale.

 
 How You'll Work:

 
 Project Ownership: Own projects from start to finish and architect solutions on your own with limited guidance. This could look like talking to customers, scoping out a solution, and then building, testing, and documenting it — all within a few days!

 Full Stack Development: Work across the entire tech stack, writing backend, frontend, and LLM system code.

 Complete Autonomy: You’ll be on the cutting edge of a developing technology space. Experimentation and rapid iteration aren’t just encouraged, they’re expected!

 
 This is a unique opportunity to shape the future of the housing industry while working with the latest advancements in AI. You'll be building something truly novel – not just another API wrapper around ChatGPT, but sophisticated systems that can reliably automate complex human workflows.

 What We are Looking for

 
 
 Experience : At least 2 years of hands-on experience with software engineering. Alternatively, 2+ years working more generally in AI/ML.

 
 Versatility : You are comfortable working at every level of the stack; from writing backend and frontend code, to designing LLM chains, to implementing necessary tools or processes.

 
 Autonomy : You don’t require heavy direction, enjoy open-ended problems, and thrive in the face of uncertainty – typical elements of a startup environment.

 
 Decision-Making Skills : Ability to make quick technical decisions and clearly communicate your decision-making process.

 
 Communication Skills : You’re clear and concise in explaining your thought process and in enumerating the tradeoffs made in your work.

 
 Relocation : Open to relocating to NYC.

 
 Passion for LLMs : You keep up with the latest developments in AI/LLMs in your spare time and are eager to apply these to solve real-world problems.

 
 Bonus : Familiarity with emerging prompting techniques and familiarity building agents over different modalities (voice, email, SMS).

 
 Bonus : Experience in building AI agents (e.g., with langchain's/llamaindex’s agents, memory, and chain modules). We want to see what you’ve built!

 
 Technology Stack

 Frontend

 
 Vue JS

 Vite

 Tailwind

 
 Backend

 
 NodeJS

 Express

 Typescript

 MongoDB

 
 ML

 
 GPT, Claude, Gemini, and Llama family of models

 Embeddings

Note: This role is exclusively available to U.S. citizens who can obtain and maintain a U.S. government-issued Top Secret security clearance with full-scope polygraph. There are no exceptions.

 Who We Are: 

 Our mission at Hazel is simple and powerful. We use AI to help U.S. government purchasing teams buy at the speed of need with unparalleled efficiency and cost-effectiveness. It is a $2.7T problem every single year, and Hazel is at the forefront with Federal and State, Local, and EDucation (SLED) agencies.  

 Hazel has identified a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to solve this by deploying cutting-edge AI to the public sector and unlocking the full potential of our nation. We empower our partners to move with expediency against the most pressing challenges of this century. Hazel builds AI for the public interest.

 What We Are Building: 

 Hazel is transforming government procurement: how government agencies purchase goods and services from commercial vendors. You’ll work directly with our federal partners in deploying our AI-forward bid evaluations solution, helping them assess vendors more accurately, reliably, and quickly. With the federal government under increasing pressure to accelerate acquisitions, our tools give them a real edge.

 Our full COTS platform supports the entire procurement lifecycle from need identification through award management, and our other partners include state & local governments, schools, and special authorities.

 About the Role: 

 As a Hazel full stack engineer, you will be at the helm of architecting and building Hazel’s platform. You will work closely with Hazel’s customers to identify pain points to prioritize the most impactful features.  You will build new features every week and be faced with many challenging technical problems. Most importantly, you join the tip of the spear in delivering effective and secure AI for the public sector.

 This specific role is for deploying and scaling Hazel's cutting-edge AI for our largest contract with one of the most important U.S. government agencies. You will bring it into production by owning the customization, security accreditation, and deployment of Hazel in a unique operational environment. 

 What You’ll Do Day-to-Day: 

 
 Define, iterate, and execute on projects contributing to Hazel’s federal and commercial product roadmap 

 Identify areas for process and technical improvements, and take the initiative to enhance engineering processes and structures in classified environments 

 Collaborate with colleagues, customers, and partners to understand key tactical and strategic priorities and deliver innovative, impactful solutions 

 Take ownership of projects, ensuring they meet rigorous standards of quality, performance, and security accreditation for a classified deployment 

 Work onsite in Washington D.C. to deploy Hazel onto classified networks

 
 What We’re Looking For: 

 
 Proven experience as a full stack engineer or similar role 

 Proven experience with the technologies in our Tech Stack, which includes:
 
 Designing and implementing AWS cloud infrastructure 

 Python 

 Typescript 

 React/Next.js  

 PostgreSQL  

 CI/CD tools (e.g., GitHub, CodePipeline) 

 IaC (CDK/Terraform) 

 Familiarity with Auth N/Auth Z/SAML/SSO/IdP 

 Docker

 


 Adeptness with using AI Coding Tools (e.g. Cursor/Claude Code) 

 Willingness to spend 25% of time onsite with customers (all travel is covered) 

 Excitement about bringing innovation to the public sector 

 Continuous drive towards learning and personal development  

 TS/SCI with FSP 
 
 We will sponsor new clearances

 


 
 Nice to Have Experience: 

 
 Has worked at a startup or startup-like environment 

 Experience working in air-gapped or classified environments 

 Security accreditation (e.g., IL-6, ICD-503) 

 AWS Solutions Architect or similar 

 Building or contributing to “from scratch” applications that had many users 

 Mapping business priorities to technical priorities and vice versa 

 Working with or adjacent to the public sector

 
 Base Salary Range: 

 $150K - $200K USD | 0.25% - 0.75% Company Equity 

 
 Estimate provided is dependent on a wide range of factors, including years of experience, relevant knowledge, and current security clearance 

 Generous health & 401(k) benefits 

 Relocation Assistance 

 Unlimited PTO

 
 Why Join Hazel: 

 This is Hazel’s first engagement in a new customer portfolio. We are one of the very first startups to ever win work with this customer. You will lead this product deployment. If you are interested in a career in national security, we will sponsor your Top Secret clearance for this role.

 As a Hazel engineer, you’ll architect and deploy secure, scalable systems that bring AI to government procurement. You’ll own features end-to-end, from backend services and data pipelines to model integration and front-end delivery, helping our partners move faster and smarter. Hazel combines startup speed with the mission focus of public service.

 You’ll work side-by-side with inquisitive and driven founders as well as a world-class and dynamic founding engineering team. Hazel continuously promotes learning and provides numerous resources from our founders, other startups, and VCs – as well as frontline exposure to customer executives and multiple engineering skillsets.

 Hazel is a mission-driven organization founded by engineers and built around delivering world-class innovation to our government partners. We strive for excellence, deliver impact, and have fun doing it.

 How You Fit Us: 

 You care about mission. Hazel partners with agencies supporting everything from public school teachers in the classroom to warfighters in the field. We believe in products which change America for the better. 

 You want a startup that is structured without being rigid, quality-obsessed without being slow, and product-led without losing the vision. You take ownership: shaping the roadmap, talking to customers, and wearing many hats. We build exceptional generalists. 

 You solve problems. There’s no more time to opine on strategy and policy when our nation faces existential challenges, today. We are workhorses, not showhorses. 

 You are always learning. Nobody has all the answers, and the world is ever-evolving, so we must be adaptive. We bring the best practices available from all domains to meet the challenge. 

 You believe in team. Our team grows together, supports one another, and elevates our shared successes. We are more effective together.

 Disclosures: 

 Hazel is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status, age, or any other federally protected class.

 Hazel’s name is inspired by Hazel Ying Lee, one of the first Chinese-American women to serve in the U.S. military as a member of the Women Airforce Service Pilots in World War II. All Americans can break barriers, dare to innovate, and be the change.

Product Manager

 We're hiring a Product Manager to help us build the future of automated document workflows at Heron. You'll own a product vertical end-to-end - from customer discovery to shipping features that eliminate hours of manual work for our customers every day. If you love talking to customers, shipping fast, and working at the intersection of AI and financial services, we'd love to hear from you.

 The Opportunity 

 Every day, thousands of small businesses apply for funding. A broker emails over a stack of bank statements, tax returns, and application forms - sometimes scanned, screenshotted or photographed on someone's phone. A team of underwriters then spends hours doing the same manual review, over and over, all day, out of a shared inbox. If they're slow, they lose the deal to a competitor.

 Heron automates that manual work. We read the emails, classify the documents, extract and enrich the data, run evaluation rules, and push decision-ready output straight into the customer's CRM - in minutes, not hours. 150+ funders, brokers, fintechs and insurers use us in production today. We're growing fast, with customers pulling us into new problems and verticals faster than we can staff them.

 We're hiring a Product Manager to take ownership of one of our core verticals. You'll talk to customers, define what to build, ship it with engineering, and measure whether it's working. You'll have real influence over the roadmap and the autonomy to make calls. This is a hands-on IC role - no layers between you and the problem.

 What You'll Do 

 
 
 Own a product vertical end-to-end: Run discovery, write specs, prioritize the backlog, and work with engineering to ship improvements that customers love

 
 Be the voice of the customer: Build deep relationships with customers in your vertical, run regular feedback sessions, and translate their pain points into product opportunities

 
 Ship and iterate: Move fast, measure impact, and iterate. We care about outcomes, not output - a shipped feature that moves a metric beats a perfect spec that sits in a backlog

 
 Set product strategy: Build and present a clear product strategy grounded in customer data, competitive insight, and business impact

 
 Collaborate across the company: Work closely with engineering, deployment, sales, and customer success to make sure what we build lands well

 
 What We're Looking For 

 
 2-4 years of experience in product management at a B2B SaaS company, or 2-3 years in a commercial generalist role before transitioning into tech or product

 You've shipped software products and can point to specific outcomes you drove

 You're comfortable with technical concepts - you can discuss APIs, data pipelines, and system architecture with engineers

 You're a structured thinker who can take ambiguous problems and break them into clear, actionable product decisions

 You have strong customer instincts, and you'd generally rather spend an hour with a customer than an hour in a planning meeting

 
 Above all else, we value people who want to make an impact in their work. Here are some of the behaviors that we expect of all Herons:

 
 
 Scrappy and creative : when faced with an obstacle, you will either overcome it by pure determination (i.e., by hand), or find an unconventional approach or ‘hack’ to navigate it.

Senior Product Manager (Platform)

 We're hiring a Senior Product Manager to own and shape the platform at Heron: the core infrastructure, tooling, and systems that underpin everything we do for customers. If you want to define the technical foundation of a fast-growing AI company and work closely with world-class engineers, we'd love to hear from you.

 The Opportunity 

 Every day, thousands of small businesses apply for funding. A broker emails over a stack of bank statements, tax returns, and application forms. A team of underwriters then spends hours on manual review. Heron automates that work: we read the emails, classify the documents, extract and enrich the data, run evaluations, and push decision-ready output straight into the customer's systems.

 150+ funders, brokers, fintechs and insurers use us in production today. As we scale across verticals and geographies, the platform that powers all of this needs a dedicated product leader. That's where you come in.

 You'll own the product vision for Heron's platform: our workflow automation engine (affectionately known as Flowmingo), core data infrastructure, deployment tooling, and the abstractions that let our product teams ship confidently. You'll work directly with our platform engineers to define what to build and why. This is a deeply technical product role; it’s less about customer-facing UIs and much more about the systems and tooling that make everything else possible.

 What You'll Do 

 
 
 Own platform product strategy: Define the vision for Heron's platform: what it is, what it can be, and what it should be. Translate that into a clear, prioritised roadmap

 
 Drive ‘Flowmingo’ forward: Own the product direction for our workflow automation engine, enabling templated deployments and scalable customer onboarding

 
 Work with senior engineers: Provide product direction to a strong backend engineering team. You'll need to earn their trust through technical credibility and sharp product thinking

 
 Build foundations for scale: Build the platform capabilities that our current and future customer vertical teams depend on: data modeling, infrastructure, billing, and developer experience

 
 Ship(!): Move fast, measure impact, and make pragmatic trade-offs.

 4-7 years of product management experience, with meaningful time owning a platform, infrastructure, or developer tools product

job_description_text:
Heron processes millions of sensitive financial documents for some of the fastest-growing MCA Funders, Brokers, Insurers, and Fintechs in the US and UK. We're at a point where the volume, velocity, and complexity of the data flowing through our systems and is outgrowing the way they were originally built. We need someone who's done this before: taken a platform that works and made it scale.

 The Challenge

 We've built a document automation and workflow orchestration platform that lenders, funders and fintechs rely on to make real decisions. It's working, customers love it, and we're onboarding new ones faster than ever.

 The challenge is that our backend was built for speed-to-market, and now we need to make it built for scale. Concretely, that means:

 
 
 Abstractions that let us move fast : The backend systems and patterns that let a 15-person engineering team ship product confidently without breaking things for existing customers.

 
 Multi-tenancy at scale : Dozens of enterprise customers sharing the same cloud infrastructure, each with different data sensitivity requirements, SLAs, and integration patterns.

 
 Data volume and velocity : High-throughput processing of financial documents (bank statements, tax returns, pay stubs) with ML pipelines that need to be fast, accurate, and reliable.

 
 Security and compliance : Sensitive financial data handled to exacting standards.

 
 We’re excited about this challenge because it is the kind of problem that shows up when things are going well! We're hiring this role because we couldn’t be more confident in our trajectory but need to build the foundations for the next 10x.

 What You'll Own

 This is a hands-on, high-autonomy IC role. You'll work directly with our CTO (Dom) and Head of Engineering (Oli), and you'll have real ownership over the systems that matter most.

 In your first few months, you'll likely:

 
 Assess our current backend architecture and identify the highest-leverage areas for improvement around reliability, scalability, and security.

 Partner with the platform and infrastructure team to design and implement the core abstractions (data model, data pipeline reliability, deployment patterns) that will support our next phase of growth.

 Set the technical direction for how we scale our infrastructure from handling hundreds of customers to thousands, and from millions of documents to tens of millions.

 Raise the bar on backend engineering practices across the team: not through process for process's sake, but by building systems that make the right thing the easy thing.

 
 Longer-term, this role shapes how Heron's entire platform evolves. You'll be the person who ensures our backend can support whatever product throws at it.

 Who Thrives Here

 The best person for this role has likely spent 8+ years building and scaling backend systems, with at least a couple of meaningful "at-bats" taking a system from early stage to serious scale. You've probably worked in B2B SaaS and have opinions (earned the hard way) about how to build systems that are both reliable and fast to develop on.

 More specifically:

 
 You have a strong CS foundation and are deeply technical. You write code, review architectures, and debug production issues.

 You've seen and built backend systems scale at companies growing from Series A to Series C+ (roughly $5M to $100M+ ARR). You know what breaks and when.

 You have recent production experience with Python and cloud infrastructure.

 You've dealt with sensitive data at volume: financial, health, or similarly regulated domains.

 You're an IC at heart. You might set direction and mentor others, but you want to build, not manage.

 You care about getting things right but also about shipping. We're a startup. Pragmatism matters.

 
 What You Can Expect as a Heron 

 
 Competitive compensation, including equity

 Based full-time in London or NYC, where most of the team is in-person multiple days per week

 Your choice of top-of-the-line equipment to fit out your desk in the office

 Occasional travel for retreats and to work with the team

 Competitive benefits: health insurance; pension/401k; uncapped annual leave; parental leave

 Free mental health support via Spill - on-demand access to therapy

 Weekly company lunches (always inclusive and vegan-friendly - especially if you like Korean or Vietnamese food!)

 Applicants from any background and gender are welcome and we have a zero tolerance policy on discrimination

Hindsight helps GTM teams understand why they win or lose business. We work with top-tier scale ups and F500 companies to deliver insights, better messaging, and personalized coaching using AI. Our customers love us and we’re ready to build a growth engine.

 We’re hiring a GTM Lead to own go-to-market end-to-end: from messaging, to content, to events. You’ll work closely with top operators across Product Marketing, Sales Enablement, and RevOps to understand how they use Hindsight - and communicate that value to the broader market.

 What You’ll Do

 
 Own messaging, positioning, and GTM narrative for our AI products

 Lead demand gen across content, website, email, and social

 Create high-quality content that drives mindshare and qualified inbound

 Partner with founders on product marketing for launches and sales enablement

 Run customer interviews and synthesize insights into GTM strategy

 Drive measurable growth in MQLs and SQLs

 
 You’re a Fit If You

 
 Have experience in product marketing, growth, or GTM roles

 Can market technical and AI-driven SaaS products

 Write clearly, move fast, and enjoy full ownership

 Want equity and the ability to shape a category early

Interfere is building the self-healing layer of the internet: software that detects issues, diagnoses root causes, and ships fixes before anyone notices something’s broken. The goal is to empower founders, product managers, and designers to understand how the product they're building actually works in the real world. Software runs civilization. When it breaks, the world stutters. We're building its immune system. This is not a normal job – it demands taste, intensity, and obsession. Difficulty wakes the genius.

 The Moment 

 AI has democratized shipping code, but excellence remains scarce. We're creating the infrastructure that makes product excellence no longer aspirational but inevitable and expected. We've raised over $5.1M from world class investors like Y Combinator, Vercel Ventures, Hummingbird, Designer Fund, and many others to turn our vision into reality. This isn't about incremental improvements, but about fundamentally changing how reliable software gets built.

 The Role 

 As a Founding Engineer, you will own end-to-end features that define how the greatest teams build and iterate upon their products. You'll architect and ship the foundational systems powering our proprietary data pipeline and interfaces that push the boundaries of what's possible.

 The Work 

 You'll operate at the intersection of the impossible and the inevitable. This role demands:

 
 
 Depth over breadth – You'll go deeper into problems than most think necessary, finding elegance where others accept complexity

 
 Craft at velocity – Ship exceptional work quickly without compromising on quality that matters. Both form and function, never just one or the other

 
 "High Standards Win" – Question everything, rebuild from scratch when needed, never accept "that's how it's done" or "good enough"

 
 Ownership without borders – Your domain is whatever needs doing to make Interfere exceptional. No excuses

 
 What You'll Build

 
 Core detection and diagnosis systems that understand codebases at scale

 Developer-facing interfaces that make complex systems feel intuitive

 Infrastructure that ships fixes autonomously and safely

 APIs and integrations that meet developers where they are

 The foundation of a platform that will handle billions of events

 
 What We're Looking For 

 
 
 Immense pride in the work you do and a desire to build the best products on earth

 
 Builders with taste – You know the difference between good and great, and you can't sleep until you've achieved the latter

 
 Systems thinkers – You see patterns others miss

 
 Relentless learners – You dive into unfamiliar domains and emerge with either expertise or a delusional drive to acquire it

 
 Clear communicators – Complex ideas become seem obvious when they find their way into your hands

 
 Domain experts – True fluency & expertise in Next.js, TypeScript, and modern JS development tooling.

 
 Nice to Have 

 
 Experience with developer tools, observability platforms, or infrastructure

 Deep knowledge of TypeScript/Node.js, React, or distributed systems

 Track record of being on a team that shipped products developers love

 Portfolio of work that demonstrates exceptionality - open source contributions, side projects, or production systems you've built

 
 What We Offer 

 
 Founding team equity and competitive compensation

 Complete ownership of meaningful work

 Health, dental, and vision coverage

 Visa sponsorship for exceptional international candidates

 The opportunity to define the technical foundation of a category-defining company

 
 How We Work 

 We're in-person in New York City, and believe proximity accelerates everything that matters, but we'll gladly help you move here. This is applied AI at its most consequential - where model performance directly determines whether production systems stay online.

 Detection systems that identify anomalies and failures across millions of codebases

 Evaluation frameworks that measure what matters - real-world reliability, not benchmark scores

 Infrastructure that serves AI models at scale with latency that doesn't break user experience

 Immense pride in the work you do and a desire to build the best products on earth

job_description_text:
Who we are 

 Keye is building the diligence layer private equity has always needed and never had. Today it does the work of an analyst, at the bar of an investment committee, on the clock of a two-week deal — 80% of the work in under five minutes, against a process that otherwise takes weeks. Firms use it to kill bad deals earlier and save millions in third-party diligence costs. Tomorrow it watches the deal after you sign. Benchmarks it against every comp in the market. Alerts you before the thesis breaks.

 Not a faster analyst. A second brain with a hundred years of scars and a live pulse on every industry it touches — built by a team that's been on the other side of the table. Four acquisitions as early employees. Twenty-plus years in M&A investing. Engineers out of Tesla, Goldman, Vista, and McKinsey, with technical DNA from AI robotics, drug discovery, and ecommerce.

 Top-10 PE funds already run diligence on Keye. $1.4T+ in AUM on the platform. 600% growth in six months. Backed by Sorenson Capital, General Catalyst, and Y Combinator.

 What you’ll do: 

 You'll build the AI systems top PE funds run live deals on. Ingestion that structures chaotic data rooms. Agents that run deterministic analysis on top. Review surfaces that make model output trustworthy enough for an investment committee. Evals, retrieval, tool use, orchestration — held to the bar of a real deal clock.

 You'd be joining a tight engineering team where everyone is load-bearing. One week you'd be heads-down on architecture — the systems that turn raw deal files into investor-ready outputs at 100% accuracy. Another week you'd be prototyping an agent harness nobody's built before. Another week you'd be cleaning up something fragile because a top-10 PE fund is running a deal on it tomorrow morning.

 Ownership here is rotating, not absent. At any given time you'll be the person accountable for some load-bearing piece — a system, a migration, a fix — and you'll hand it off or expand it as priorities shift. You'll sit in on planning, align on sequencing, and have weight in what the next two weeks look like. Every shortcut compounds. Every good abstraction compounds harder. Customer obsession matters — their timelines, their standards, their refusal to accept 99%. The work is downstream of that pressure. You'll build things that have to meet it.

 Example duties 

 
 
 Deepen the agent layer . We view the agent as a first class citizen. Not a demo. This means harnesses, planning, subagents, compaction, tool-use design — the primitives that decide whether the system scales with complexity or collapses under it. This will require iteration, decisiveness, and staying abreast on frontier updates.The faster this layer moves, the faster the product gets sharper.

 
 Own the deterministic core . Our computation and fact gathering allows any consumer to pull any intermediate artifact from a deal, compose real analytical logic on top, and return a number an IC would defend.

 
 Build evals that actually catch regressions before customers do. Not vibes. Real test harnesses for agent output, extraction accuracy, and analysis correctness.

 
 Harden the ingestion layer so it handles data rooms with unknown schemas, malformed files, and edge cases that only show up in real deals.

 
 Own the infrastructure that keeps the platform fast under real load. Concurrency, queueing, caching, cost — the systems work that decides whether we can run ten deals in parallel or ten thousand.

 
 Location: Hybrid or Remote (North America).

 What we're looking for: 

 
 You think in systems and ship in days. You know when to build the abstraction and when to skip it.

 You've succeeded at something hard and can point to it. A company you started. A team you led. A product people used. A bar you cleared that most didn't.

 You've built and operated production systems that broke, and you fixed them. Not prototypes. Not side projects. Real consequences.

 Broad across the stack. No layer is someone else's problem.

 Languages/tools: Python/React shop. We care less about stack fluency than systems instincts across the stack.

 Obsessive about testing. E2E, integration, browser — you don't ship without them, and you don't trust anyone who does.

 A point of view on the current agent landscape, and a sense of what's real vs. demo-ware.

 Previous founders encouraged

About Keye 

 Keye (YC F24) is building the world’s smartest private equity analyst. We’re not just accelerating due diligence—we’re helping investors generate alpha. Unlike typical ChatGPT-enhanced tools, Keye is designed specifically for the hard problems in PE diligence.

 When firms analyze deals through Keye, they tap into a deep library of diligence knowledge and best practices, built from over a decade of real investor experience. Our proprietary analysis engine helps teams quickly identify high-potential deals and move faster on the ones that matter.

 We’re a team of investors and engineers with backgrounds at Vista Equity Partners, Permira, Goldman Sachs, McKinsey, Tesla and multiple startups. Our founding team has over 20 years of M&A investing experience, has founded companies before, and has been part of four successful acquisitions as early employees.

 Keye is revenue-generating and has grown 600% in the last six months ( some press about our growth here ). We’re backed by top-tier investors including Y Combinator, General Catalyst, Sorenson Capital, and Plug and Play.

 Launched just four months ago, Keye is already working with 40% of the top 25 PE firms, representing over $1.7 trillion in AUM. Simply put we are a rocket ship.

 
 The Role 

 We’re hiring a Founding Strategist – Private Equity to lead the full lifecycle of client engagement—from initial demo to platform activation and expansion. Your goal is to drive product fit with each of our ICP clients. This is a technical role that requires prior experience as an investor in private equity. The position is in-person in New York (relocation support available). 

 You’ll work closely with our CEO, COO, sales, and product teams to lead platform adoption, drive activation, and turn user feedback into meaningful product improvements. You will be the face of Keye to our clients—working with them daily on real data, live deals, and high-stakes diligence.

 This is a client-first, product-critical, and founder-level role. You’ll own core relationships, build new processes, and help shape the team from the ground up.

 __________________________________________________________________________________________________________

 What You’ll Do 

 
 Drive customer activation—ensuring firms not only onboard, but fully adopt the platform

 Own relationships with clients from demo to rollout, often working directly with them on live deal data

 Run tailored POCs that showcase platform value using real, quantitative diligence processes

 Build and maintain deep, trusted relationships with senior PE professionals—across business and informal settings

 Collaborate with product and engineering to prioritize, spec, and ship features that drive usage

 Turn PE workflows and client feedback into actionable roadmap decisions

 Help define the future structure of the platform strategy org—spanning engagement, success, and support

 
 
 What We’re Looking For 

 
 2–6 years in Private Equity (investing role).

 Deep experience with analyses carried out during due diligence (beyond just commercial DD)

 Confident presenter with experience engaging senior stakeholders

 Entrepreneurial, process-driven, with strong product sense and ability to work across teams

 Works with urgency, clarity, and a strong sense of ownership

 Socially confident—comfortable leading client sessions, dinners, and events

 Based in NYC or open to relocating

 Comfortable in high-pressure, fast-paced environments with tight deadlines and demanding clients

 
 
 Why Now 

 
 Lead and define a core function at a breakout AI startup

 Work closely with the founding team to shape strategy and product

 Build and lead a future team of platform strategists, engagement managers, and sales engineers

 Own relationships with top-tier PE firms at the forefront of digital transformation

 Be part of a generational shift in how PE firms use technology

 
 
 You’ll be joining a passionate, high-energy team that’s relentless about building something industry-defining. If you’re looking to leave investing and actually build—this is your role

About Kilvin 

 Kilvin is an AI-native software agency that builds custom internal tools for non-technical teams. We deliver everything through our platform : a shared workspace for our clients to access and interact with their software. We believe strongly in the principle of personal software : technology purpose-built for the individual, and that AI-enabled software generation provides the greatest opportunity to bring personal software to the masses.

 Founded by Chris and Jorrie - MIT CS grads and former Googlers.

 The role 

 We are able to deliver faster than traditional agencies in thanks to our proprietary build pipeline and hosted workspace platform that's been battle-tested in client deployments across healthcare, finance, and operations applications. We're now investing in the next iteration of that platform : transforming it into a self-serve tool for a more technical audience.

 As a Senior Platform Engineer, you'll own core pieces of that system. You'll work directly with our CTO and Head of Engineering on the infrastructure that turns "we need a tool for X" into a running, integrated, access-controlled application. This is a high-ownership role: small team, real users from day one, and decisions that shape the product for years.

 What you'll work on 

 
 
 The app runtime and workspace - how custom apps are deployed, isolated, and served to clients inside a single secure environment.

 
 The build & deploy pipeline - the AI-assisted system that takes a user request from requirements to specs to software takes requirements to working software.

 
 Multi-tenancy and access control - tenant isolation, fine-grained permissions (who can open which app and see which data), and the auth model underneath it.

 
 Integrations - a framework for connecting client apps to the CRMs, spreadsheets, email, and databases they already rely on, so data moves cleanly between systems.

 
 Reliability & observability - monitoring, performance, and the tooling that lets a small team run many production deployments with confidence.

 
 What we're looking for 

 
 Experience with LLM orchestration or AI-assisted code generation in production.

 3+ years building and operating production software, with meaningful time spent on platform, infrastructure, or backend systems.

 Strong fundamentals in distributed systems, data modeling, and API design.

 Experience with multi-tenancy, auth/permissions, or sandboxing.

 Comfort owning systems end to end: you can design it, ship it, operate it, and debug it in production.

 A bias toward shipping. You're energized by a fast build–test–iterate loop and real users, not by long specs.

 
 Nice to have 

 
 Experience at an early-stage startup or as an early/founding engineer.

 Background in regulated domains (healthcare, finance) and the compliance that comes with them.

 You've built developer-facing or internal platforms that other people built on top of.

 
 Our stack 

 We care more about strong engineers who are comfortable with AI-assisted development workflows; if you've gone deep somewhere adjacent, we want to talk.

 
 We work in TypeScript end to end, maintaining a single monorepo spanning frontend, backend, and CLI tooling.

 Our frontend is React ; the backend is Node.js services on Express , talking to PostgreSQL through Prisma .

 Auth and sessions run on a modern auth library, and the whole platform is deployed to AWS , with infrastructure managed as code in Terraform .

 Our build-and-deploy pipeline leans heavily on modern LLM tooling to turn requirements into working software.

 
 Logistics 

 
 
 Location: Remote within the US. We have a strong preference for candidates in or near NYC, Denver, or Philadelphia for periodic in-person collaboration.

 
 Compensation: Competitive salary plus meaningful early-stage equity. We offer health benefits through a QSEHRA: a tax-free monthly reimbursement toward your health insurance premiums and qualified medical expenses. We're happy to discuss specifics early in the process.

At Kyber, we're building the next-generation document platform for enterprises. Today, our AI-native solution transforms regulatory document workflows, enabling insurance claims organizations to consolidate 80% of their templates, spend 65% less time drafting, and compress overall communication cycle times by 5x. Our vision is for every enterprise to seamlessly leverage AI templates to generate every document.

 Over the past 18 months, we’ve:

 
 >40x’d revenue and are profitable.

 Landed multiple six and seven figure, multi-year contracts with leading insurance enterprises.

 Launched strategic partnerships with industry leading software partners like Guidewire, Majesco, and Twilio Sendgrid.

 
 Kyber is backed by top Silicon Valley VCs, including Y Combinator and Fellows Fund.

 We're seeking a hands-on Head of Engineering with a clear line of sight to CTO. This role is ideal for someone who is already operating as a 10x engineer, thrives in early stage environments, and is excited to design and scale mission-critical AI systems from first principles.

 
 Responsibilities: 

 
 
 Be the Technical Owner of the Product 
 
 Own end-to-end technical decisions across backend, frontend, data, infra.

 Ship features personally while keeping the team unblocked.

 


 
 Prioritize an Aggressive Product Roadmap 
 
 Lead sprint/weekly planning and keep priorities tight

 Protect focus: decisively manage tradeoffs, push back when needed

 


 
 Unlock Leverage to Boost Engineering Capacity 
 
 Scale adoption of Agentic AI coding tools (Cursor, Claude Code, Greptile) into large scale engineering and product workflows to accelerate time to market.

 Establish reusable patterns and interfaces so engineers and agents aren’t reinventing everything.

 


 
 Champion Reliability, Security, and Customer Trust 
 
 Own uptime, performance, and incident response.

 Implement monitoring/alerts + fast debugging workflows.

 


 
 Mentor & Raise the Engineering Bar 
 
 Set expectations for quality and ownership.

 Help recruit and evaluate future hires.

 


 
 
 What We’re Looking For in You: 

 
 
 10x Engineer with a love of building 
 
 Your raw coding ability should be unmatched and only deeply amplified by AI.

 You love to build and relish seeing customers delighted by your work.

 


 
 Ship first, optimize and harden later 
 
 You get something out as quickly as possible, prove it was the right thing to build, and then optimize and harden it.

 


 
 System Design Mastery 
 
 You've architected large scale data-driven applications and have a full E2E understanding of what tradeoffs to make when.

 


 
 Systems-first thinking 
 
 Not just in architecture, but also for how an AI-native engineering team will operate as we continue to grow.

 


 
 Accountability 
 
 You're prepared and have been in roles before where you've had to hold other engineers accountable for their deliverables.

 


 
 Security and Compliance 
 
 You've dealt with enterprise software compliance requirements (SOC 2, HIPAA, ISO, etc) and are comfortable incorporating those requirements in tandem with product build out.

 


 
 
 Our Values 

 
 
 Possible Until Proven Otherwise 
 
 Challenge assumptions with evidence. If it’s impossible, show us why.

 


 
 LOVE Your Customer 
 
 Put customers at the heart of everything. Earn trust, deliver value, grow together.

 


 
 Take Pride In Your Craft 
 
 Creating something from nothing is a privilege—embrace the process and perfect the details.

 


 
 Live Up To Your Expectations 
 
 Set your standards high and let’s exceed them together—because no one should expect more of you than yourself.

 


 
 Have Fun & Nurture Those Around You 
 
 The joy of building is amplified when shared. Remember to support, uplift, and celebrate each other as we grow.

 


 
 
 Benefits 

 
 Competitive salary

 Generous stock package

 100% employer-covered medical, dental, and vision insurance

 
 
 Why Kyber? 

 Join us in building and scaling a game-changing enterprise product powered by state-of-the-art AI. At Kyber, your contributions will directly impact how businesses handle some of their most critical workflows and customer interactions.

 If you’re obsessed with building, AI, and transforming enterprise workflows, we’d love to hear from you!

 How To Stand Out 

 We want to hear from extraordinary individuals who are ready to shape the future of enterprise documents. To stand out, ask someone you’ve worked with to send your resume or LinkedIn profile, along with a brief 2-3 sentence endorsement, directly to arvind [at] askkyber.com .

 Referrals matter. They help us understand the impact you’ve already had and the kind of teammate you’ll be. A strong referee can elevate your application, so choose someone who knows your skills and character well.

 Apply today and help us bring enterprise documents into the AI-native age.

 Enjoy working closely with senior leadership and taking ownership of meaningful outcomes..

 
 Bonus Points If You Have:

 Comfort working with data, financial models, and operational analyses.

 
 Why You’ll Love Working Here:

 Work directly with the leadership team on the most important initiatives facing the company.

 Fully remote work environment built on autonomy and accountability

LANDED makes Hiring Managers happy, saving companies time and money by automating candidate sourcing, engagement, and interview scheduling.  As a Product Manager, you will play a direct role in shaping the strategic direction of our product, with a deep focus on Generative AI, Systems, and UI/UX. You'll work closely with cross-functional teams including Engineering, Marketing, Sales, Customer Success, and Operations. This role will touch unique AI applications, technical solutions architecture, integration strategies, and implementation of our SaaS product.

 
 Who you are 

 You thrive on innovation and building what’s next. You’ve seen how successful companies operate, but also have scrappiness in your DNA. The successful candidate will be highly motivated with a high degree of focus on detail, process and creativity. 
 
 Key Responsibilities: 

 
 
 Product & Project Management: 
 
 Take ownership of the product lifecycle from conception to launch including ideation, coordination, stakeholder management, and measurement of product performance

 Continually refine workflows, methodologies, and deployment processes to amplify efficiency and quality

 Define, measure, and share the important critical metrics to measure the success

About LiveFlow 

 We are building the next-generation accounting and finance platform to enable lean finance teams to run massive enterprises.

 We have raised over $21M from top-tier investors including: YC , YC Continuity , Valar Seedcamp , WndrCo , Moonfire, Bradley Horowitz (VP Product, Google) and more.

 We are helping thousands of companies to streamline their financial workflows and we have hundreds of 5/5 ratings on G2. 

 Our founding team previously worked at Europe's fastest growing fintech, Revolut. 

 🚀 WHY JOIN US? 

 You'll help shape your own role here as a function of your talent, your passion, and your ambition.

 We want you to work smart, bring your own perspective, and ask deep questions about what we do and how we do it. You'll play a vital role in how we foster an inspiring culture, and in how we scale the organization in the coming years.

 You love solving problems, right? That's why you're here. Well, we've got excited customers, eagerly awaiting your ideas and implementations. We've got tons of interesting challenges ahead – both technical and non-technical – and we definitely don't have all the answers yet, but we're striving to ask better questions every day.

 Do you relish in the delight of customers, and in the opportunity to develop an amazing product you'll be proud of, and one they'll be happy to pay for? Do you have a keen eye for detail and design, but enjoy trading off perfection in pursuit of progress? Do you thrive in an environment of quick and collaborative iteration? If you're answering yes to those questions, we'd love to hear from you.

 SOME OF THE TECH CHALLENGES YOU'LL HELP US MASTER INCLUDE: 

 
 
  Building a real-time, collaborative financial workspace for teams



 
  Designing and developing a scalable and maintainable API



 
  Integrating with lots of different financial data providers



 
  Crafting beautifully simple abstractions to enable adaptable, frictionless UX



 
  Handling our own and customers' data securely and compliantly



 
 OUR STACK: 

 On the backend, we've got an Elixir/Phoenix application, deployed on AWS via Terraform. On the frontend, we have a NextJS application using TypeScript.

 We don't expect you to be an expert in every one of those areas.  

 If you've enjoyed working in any functional language, we think you'll love Elixir (but if you've never even dabbled, we think you'll love it too). If you've felt productive working with a framework like Rails or Django, you'll get the hang of Phoenix in no time.

 If you've worked with React, but not TypeScript, we can work with that. If you know your JS, but have never written React, that's fine too. You get the picture – it's not about your familiarity with specific technologies. If you're an expert in one or another domain, but open to learning as you go, we'll start from there.

 Over time, we'll expect you to wear many hats. You'll be an architect balancing performance and pragmatism. You'll be a developer considering and implementing different designs. You'll be a product manager cutting and simplifying features. You'll be a leader and a mentor.

 You're the kind of developer who knows not only what the best practices are, but understands why they are, and so knows when to ignore them. You're confident in making your case but open to alternative ideas, and willing to disagree and commit either way.

 PERKS AND BENEFITS: 

 
 
 Competitive base salary and equity



 
 Health insurance



 
 Pension plan



 
 All the latest tech you need; MacBooks, monitors, etc.



 
 Monthly budget to spend on books and conferences to keep learning



 
 Unlimited vacation policy



 
 HOW DOES THE INTERVIEW PROCESS LOOK LIKE? 

 
 
 Intro call



 
 Technical Assessment



 
 Technical interview



 
 Career History Interview



 
 Founder's Interview

About LiveFlow

 We’re LiveFlow - one of the fastest-growing fintech startups in the world. Backed by Y Combinator and $21M from top-tier investors including YC, YC Continuity, Valar, Seedcamp, WndrCo, Moonfire, and Bradley Horowitz (VP Product at Google), we’re building the next-generation platform that helps finance teams operate at their best.

 We’re already trusted by thousands of companies worldwide and consistently maintain hundreds of 5/5 ratings on G2. Our founding team previously helped scale Revolut - Europe’s fastest-growing fintech.

 Now it’s your chance to join us at the ground floor.

 Why Join the Platform Team as a Graduate Engineer?

 If you’re fresh out of university (or graduated in the last couple of years) and want to kick-start your career in tech, this is the place.

 At LiveFlow, you won’t just write code - you’ll be part of a fast-paced, global team building real systems used by thousands of companies around the world.

 You’ll learn by doing, working alongside experienced engineers while gaining exposure to modern infrastructure, internal tooling, and real-world production systems.

 Why LiveFlow

 • Be part of a rocketship fintech 
• Learn directly from world-class engineers who’ve built products at scale 
• Get exposure to a wide range of technologies and real engineering challenges 
• Work on systems that support both our product and internal teams 
• Shape our culture, product, and processes as we grow 
• Work in a collaborative environment where curiosity and initiative are valued

 The Team You'll Work With

 You’ll be based in our London office , working closely with our Head of Platform Engineering and two Platform Engineers already based there .

 This means you’ll be surrounded by engineers who can support your learning and help you grow quickly as you start your career.

 While our platform team has a strong presence in London, our wider engineering team is globally distributed across Europe, South America, and the United States . You’ll collaborate with teammates across time zones and gain exposure to how modern distributed engineering teams operate.

 We work in a highly collaborative environment with regular pairing, code reviews, and knowledge sharing. As a graduate engineer, you’ll receive hands-on support while still having the opportunity to contribute to meaningful projects from day one.

 What You’ll Work On

 As a Graduate Platform Engineer, you’ll work on projects such as:

 • Building internal tools that improve productivity across engineering, product, and growth teams 
• Supporting the infrastructure behind our Growth Marketing Engine and Pulse platform 
• Contributing to data pipelines and integrations with external financial data providers 
• Developing APIs and backend services that support internal systems 
• Building internal AI tools that help teams automate workflows and move faster 
• Improving automation and developer tooling used across the engineering team 
• Helping ensure our systems remain secure, reliable, and scalable as we grow

 You’ll work closely with experienced engineers and gain hands-on experience with the infrastructure and systems that power a fast-growing fintech.

 Our Tech Stack

 Backend: Elixir / Phoenix on AWS (via Terraform) 
Frontend: Next.js with TypeScript 

 Don’t worry if you haven’t worked with these technologies before - we value strong fundamentals and curiosity over specific tools.

 If you’ve worked with languages like JavaScript, Python, Java, Go, or Rust , you’ll be able to pick things up quickly.

 What We’re Looking For

 • Recent graduate in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field 
• Strong interest in coding and solving technical problems 
• Curiosity about how software systems work behind the scenes 
• Experience with programming through university projects, internships, or personal projects 
• Willingness to learn new technologies quickly 
• Team spirit, creativity, and ambition to grow as an engineer

 You don’t need extensive industry experience - we’re looking for smart, curious engineers who want to learn fast and build meaningful systems.

 Perks & Benefits

 • Competitive salary 
• Equity in one of the world’s fastest-growing fintech companies 
• Health insurance & pension plan 
• Unlimited paid vacation 
• Monthly budget for books, courses, and conferences 
• MacBook and all the tech you need

 Interview Process

 • Loom Video Submission 
• Screening Call 
• Scenario Interview 
• Culture Interview 
• Final Interview

About LiveFlow

We are building the next-generation accounting and finance platform that transforms outdated systems and complex, often manual workflows into a simple, smart and intuitive user experience—and we need a brilliant designer to help us bring our vision to life.

 We have raised over $21M from top-tier investors including : YC , YC Continuity , Valar Seedcamp , WndrCo , Moonfire, Bradley Horowitz (VP Product, Google) and more.

 We are helping thousands of companies to streamline their financial workflows and we have hundreds of 5/5 ratings on G2.

 About the role 

 We're hiring a Partnerships Manager to build and run our influencer and media partnerships program.

 The role 

 You'll find, evaluate, and close partnerships with finance and accounting influencers, podcasts, newsletters, and media platforms. You'll negotiate sponsorship deals, buy ad placements, and figure out which bets are worth making.

 This role sits at the intersection of sales, marketing, and analytics. You need to be able to cold outreach a podcast host, negotiate a deal that makes sense for both sides, write ad copy that actually resonates, and then measure whether the spend was worth it.

 Role Overview 

 We’re looking for a Senior Robotics Engineer (Systems & Controls) to join our core team.

 You will design and tune the systems that make robot behavior reliable under real-world constraints, turning task intent into stable, precise, production-grade motion on real hardware. This includes controller design, dynamics and system-response analysis, industrial hardware integration, and deployment through commissioning.

 This is a hands-on role for someone equally comfortable with control design, motion behavior, hardware integration, and debugging across the full stack.

 What You’ll Do 

 
 
 Design and Tune Control Architectures: Design and tune controllers for robot motion using PID, feedforward, state-space methods, LQR, and MPC.

 
 First-Principles Modeling: Model system dynamics with block diagrams, transfer functions, and state-space methods; analyze stability, overshoot, settling time, damping, tracking error, and robustness.

 
 Motion Execution & OEM Interfacing: Build motion execution around industrial OEM controllers, accounting for latency, calibration error, joint limits, motion constraints, and fault handling.

 
 Lead System Integration: Integrate our platform with real-world industrial robotic systems across sensors, network interfaces, safety I/O, and hardware.

 
 Deploy and Commission: Own bring-up, validation, commissioning, and on-site issue resolution in production environments.

 
 Full-Stack Debugging: Debug failures across controls, software, networking, calibration, and hardware.

 
 What We’re Looking For 

 
 
 Applied Control Theory: Deep expertise in classical and modern control theory (Laplace transforms, frequency-domain analysis, LQR, MPC) with a proven track record of deploying these algorithms on physical hardware, not just in simulation.

 
 Strong Systems Intuition: A deep understanding of closed-loop system behavior, including stability, overshoot, tracking error, and robustness in messy physical environments.

 
 Hardware & Commissioning Experience: Hands-on experience integrating, tuning, and commissioning industrial robot hardware (ABB, Fanuc, KUKA, UR) in real-world or production settings.

 
 Software Engineering: 4+ years of experience in robotics software. Strong C++/Python skills, especially at the boundary between high-level task planning services and deterministic robot execution.

 
 Startup DNA: High agency, strong bias toward action, and the ability to thrive in a fast-paced, execution-focused environment.

 
 Preferred Qualifications 

 
 
 RAPID Proficiency: Deep experience programming and interfacing with ABB controllers via RAPID.

About Us 

 Metal is building the world's most powerful intelligence platform for private market investors, putting cutting-edge AI in the hands of the most sophisticated firms on the planet.

 In private markets, one thing defines success: the quality of your investment outcomes. Metal is the infrastructure-first platform built to sharpen every decision and build conviction across the full deal lifecycle, from sourcing to exit.

 We're growing fast. Top-tier firms like Berkshire Partners and Clearlake Capital already run on Metal to move faster and invest smarter. Adoption is compounding, the category is ours to define, and we're backed to win it.

 Our team is sharp, low-ego, and obsessed with the people we build for. We take ownership of hard, ambiguous problems and we move fast. If you do your best work close to the customer and want to shape how the best investors in the world operate, we'd love to meet you.

 The Role 

 Metal is redefining how private markets operate. But lasting impact means our platform has to live inside how firms run their investment process and make decisions. The Forward Deployed Investor is how we get there.

 You'll sit at the intersection of top-tier investment teams and their AI evolution, pairing deep private markets expertise with real customer empathy and a strong instinct for how AI reshapes an investment firm. You'll help customers rethink the way they work, and translate what you learn on the ground into Metal's product and go-to-market direction.

 You'll work directly with the founding team and our most strategic customers to drive adoption and ROI. You are the voice of the customer across Product and Sales, and one of the people deciding how private investors operate in an AI-driven world.

 What You'll Do 

 
 
 Define how AI is used in private markets . Identify and expand high-impact use cases across sourcing, diligence, and decision-making, and turn what works into best practices and deployment frameworks for how investment teams adopt Metal.



 
 Shape product and go-to-market strategy from the field. Translate first-hand customer insight into clear product direction, influencing Metal's roadmap, positioning, and sales motion.



 
 Drive use-case expansion and growth. Uncover new workflows, teams, and applications within customer organizations, partnering with Sales to execute expansions and deepen Metal's footprint.



 
 Build a scalable deployment model. Turn successful implementations into repeatable onboarding, enablement, and rollout frameworks that scale across customers.



 
 Own value realization for strategic customers. Ensure Metal is embedded in core investment workflows, delivering measurable adoption and ROI.



 
 Act as a trusted advisor. Partner with deal, operations, and data teams to evolve workflows over time and support long-term adoption.



 
 Develop champions and advocacy. Build durable relationships, enable internal champions, and convert successful deployments into references and market credibility.



 
 Who You'll Work With 

 
 
 Metal's founding team, including the CEO, CPO, and CTO, to shape how investment professionals engage with the platform.



 
 Cross-functional leaders across Strategy, Sales, Product, and Engineering, aligning client outcomes with product direction.



 
 C-suite executives, deal partners, and operating leaders at top private capital firms, helping them turn investment expertise into a technology-driven edge.



 
 What We're Looking For 

 
 
 You've done the work yourself. 3-5 years in private equity, growth equity, or management consulting, with hands-on deal execution and diligence. You understand the investment process front to back, down to the details that make it actually tick.



 
 You build with AI, you don't just follow it. You use AI routinely in your own work, spin up your own agents and workflow augmentations, and stay fluent in a toolkit that changes every month. You see where it goes next before most people do.



 
 Exceptional executive presence. You engage persuasively with partners, principals, and C-level stakeholders, and build relationships that last.



 
 Entrepreneurial mindset. You thrive in ambiguity, take initiative, and are motivated to build the blueprint others will follow.



 
 You want to build the category. You're excited to define a new layer of investment infrastructure, not just join an existing one.



 
 Benefits & Compensation

 Salary Range (OTE): $150,000 to $200,000 USD, depending on experience.

 Equity: Meaningful early-stage ownership in Metal.

job_title: Software Engineer, Quality [SDET] NYC/Flexible work arrangements
job_description_text:
OneChronos systems match billions of dollars in transactions daily on infrastructure where the margin for error is as thin as the markets demand. Our engineering team has scaled its output faster than most, and that growth has created a clear opportunity: to build a dedicated quality engineering function proactively -- not as a response to problems, but as the natural next step for a team firing on all cylinders.

 We rebuilt market structure from first principles. Help us build the quality platform that scales with it.

 We're looking for someone to own quality as an engineering discipline. From test frameworks and CI/CD integrations to staging environments, simulation infrastructure, and automated validation, you'll build the systems that help engineers verify correctness before code reaches production. Your mission is to increase engineering velocity and confidence by making testing reliable, scalable, and easy to use across the organization.

 About OneChronos ( www.OneChronos.com ) OneChronos is a technology company of diverse thinkers innovating at the intersection of capital markets, mechanism design, and operations research, working to grow the global GDP by designing and operating matching markets leveraging advances in auction theory and artificial intelligence. Our software and systems are developed in house and match billions of dollars per day of security transactions.

 What You'll Do 

 
 Design, build, and maintain automated test frameworks and testing infrastructure across our trading platform, covering correctness, performance, and reliability.

 Develop tooling that enables engineering teams to test their own code more effectively, without creating bottlenecks.

 Define test strategies for new features and system changes in partnership with engineering, SRE, and DevOps teams.

 Build and own CI/CD pipeline integrations that gate deployments on meaningful quality signals.

 Build testing infrastructure and validation tooling that surfaces regressions and correctness issues before they reach production.

 Investigate test failures, quality gaps, and escaped defects, driving structural improvements to prevent recurrence.

 Participate in design and architecture reviews to identify testability gaps early.

 
 Who You Are 

 
 You write production-quality code and treat test infrastructure the same way -- not as scaffolding, but as software that needs to be maintained and improved.

 You have strong experience with test automation frameworks, testing infrastructure, and developer tooling, and have built or meaningfully extended one from scratch.

 You're fluent in at least one systems-oriented language (Rust, C++, Go, or Java) and at home in Python for scripting and tooling.

 You have worked in low-latency, high-throughput, or otherwise performance-sensitive environments and understand what it means to test at that layer.

 You think in edge cases. You're the person on the team who asks "but what happens if..."

 You work fluidly across organizational lines -- with SRE on reliability, with DevOps on deployment pipelines, with product engineers on correctness.

 Financial markets or trading systems experience is a plus, not a requirement. Curiosity about the domain is.

 
 Compensation 

 Base salary range: $150,000 -- $200,000, depending on experience, with eligibility for a discretionary annual bonus.
